68#else
69/* OpenLDAP deprecates RFC 1823, but we want standard conformance */
70#define LDAP_DEPRECATED 1
71#include <ldap.h>
72typedef struct timeval LDAP_TIMEVAL;
73#endif
74static int	ldapServiceLookup(const char *purl, PQconninfoOption *options,
75							  PQExpBuffer errorMessage);
76#endif

78#ifndef WIN32
79#define PGPASSFILE ".pgpass"
80#else
81#define PGPASSFILE "pgpass.conf"
82#endif
83
84/*
85 * Pre-9.0 servers will return this SQLSTATE if asked to set
86 * application_name in a startup packet.  We hard-wire the value rather
87 * than looking into errcodes.h since it reflects historical behavior
88 * rather than that of the current code.
89 */
90#define ERRCODE_APPNAME_UNKNOWN "42704"
91
92/* This is part of the protocol so just define it */
93#define ERRCODE_INVALID_PASSWORD "28P01"
94/* This too */
95#define ERRCODE_CANNOT_CONNECT_NOW "57P03"
96
97/*
98 * Cope with the various platform-specific ways to spell TCP keepalive socket
99 * options.  This doesn't cover Windows, which as usual does its own thing.
100 */
101#if defined(TCP_KEEPIDLE)
102/* TCP_KEEPIDLE is the name of this option on Linux and *BSD */
103#define PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE TCP_KEEPIDLE
104#define PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE_STR "TCP_KEEPIDLE"
105#elif defined(TCP_KEEPALIVE_THRESHOLD)
106/* TCP_KEEPALIVE_THRESHOLD is the name of this option on Solaris >= 11 */
107#define PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE TCP_KEEPALIVE_THRESHOLD
108#define PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE_STR "TCP_KEEPALIVE_THRESHOLD"
109#elif defined(TCP_KEEPALIVE) && defined(__darwin__)
110/* TCP_KEEPALIVE is the name of this option on macOS */
111/* Caution: Solaris has this symbol but it means something different */
112#define PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE TCP_KEEPALIVE
113#define PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE_STR "TCP_KEEPALIVE"
114#endif
115
116/*
117 * fall back options if they are not specified by arguments or defined
118 * by environment variables
119 */
120#define DefaultHost		"localhost"
121#define DefaultOption	""
122#ifdef USE_SSL
123#define DefaultChannelBinding	"prefer"
124#else
125#define DefaultChannelBinding	"disable"
126#endif
127#define DefaultTargetSessionAttrs	"any"
128#define DefaultLoadBalanceHosts	"disable"
129#ifdef USE_SSL
130#define DefaultSSLMode "prefer"
131#define DefaultSSLCertMode "allow"
132#else
133#define DefaultSSLMode	"disable"
134#define DefaultSSLCertMode "disable"
135#endif
136#define DefaultSSLNegotiation	"postgres"
137#ifdef ENABLE_GSS
138#include "fe-gssapi-common.h"
139#define DefaultGSSMode "prefer"
140#else
141#define DefaultGSSMode "disable"
142#endif
143
144/* ----------
145 * Definition of the conninfo parameters and their fallback resources.
146 *
147 * If Environment-Var and Compiled-in are specified as NULL, no
148 * fallback is available. If after all no value can be determined
149 * for an option, an error is returned.
150 *
151 * The value for the username is treated specially in conninfo_add_defaults.
152 * If the value is not obtained any other way, the username is determined
153 * by pg_fe_getauthname().
154 *
155 * The Label and Disp-Char entries are provided for applications that
156 * want to use PQconndefaults() to create a generic database connection
157 * dialog. Disp-Char is defined as follows:
158 *		""		Normal input field
159 *		"*"		Password field - hide value
160 *		"D"		Debug option - don't show by default
161 *
162 * NB: Server-side clients -- dblink, postgres_fdw, libpqrcv -- use dispchar to
163 * determine which options to expose to end users, and how. Changing dispchar
164 * has compatibility and security implications for those clients. For example,
165 * postgres_fdw will attach a "*" option to USER MAPPING instead of the default
166 * SERVER, and it disallows setting "D" options entirely.
167 *
168 * PQconninfoOptions[] is a constant static array that we use to initialize
169 * a dynamically allocated working copy.  All the "val" fields in
170 * PQconninfoOptions[] *must* be NULL.  In a working copy, non-null "val"
171 * fields point to malloc'd strings that should be freed when the working
172 * array is freed (see PQconninfoFree).
173 *
174 * The first part of each struct is identical to the one in libpq-fe.h,
175 * which is required since we memcpy() data between the two!
176 * ----------
177 */
178typedef struct _internalPQconninfoOption
179{
180	char	   *keyword;		/* The keyword of the option			*/
181	char	   *envvar;			/* Fallback environment variable name	*/
182	char	   *compiled;		/* Fallback compiled in default value	*/
183	char	   *val;			/* Option's current value, or NULL		*/
184	char	   *label;			/* Label for field in connect dialog	*/
185	char	   *dispchar;		/* Indicates how to display this field in a
186								 * connect dialog. Values are: "" Display
187								 * entered value as is "*" Password field -
188								 * hide value "D"  Debug option - don't show
189								 * by default */
190	int			dispsize;		/* Field size in characters for dialog	*/
191	/* ---
192	 * Anything above this comment must be synchronized with
193	 * PQconninfoOption in libpq-fe.h, since we memcpy() data
194	 * between them!
195	 * ---
196	 */
197	off_t		connofs;		/* Offset into PGconn struct, -1 if not there */
198} internalPQconninfoOption;
199
200static const internalPQconninfoOption PQconninfoOptions[] = {
201	{"service", "PGSERVICE", NULL, NULL,
202		"Database-Service", "", 20,
203	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pgservice)},
204
205	{"user", "PGUSER", NULL, NULL,
206		"Database-User", "", 20,
207	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pguser)},
208
209	{"password", "PGPASSWORD", NULL, NULL,
210		"Database-Password", "*", 20,
211	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pgpass)},
212
213	{"passfile", "PGPASSFILE", NULL, NULL,
214		"Database-Password-File", "", 64,
215	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pgpassfile)},
216
217	{"channel_binding", "PGCHANNELBINDING", DefaultChannelBinding, NULL,
218		"Channel-Binding", "", 8,	/* sizeof("require") == 8 */
219	offsetof(struct pg_conn, channel_binding)},
220
221	{"connect_timeout", "PGCONNECT_TIMEOUT", NULL, NULL,
222		"Connect-timeout", "", 10,	/* strlen(INT32_MAX) == 10 */
223	offsetof(struct pg_conn, connect_timeout)},
224
225	{"dbname", "PGDATABASE", NULL, NULL,
226		"Database-Name", "", 20,
227	offsetof(struct pg_conn, dbName)},
228
229	{"host", "PGHOST", NULL, NULL,
230		"Database-Host", "", 40,
231	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pghost)},
232
233	{"hostaddr", "PGHOSTADDR", NULL, NULL,
234		"Database-Host-IP-Address", "", 45,
235	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pghostaddr)},
236
237	{"port", "PGPORT", DEF_PGPORT_STR, NULL,
238		"Database-Port", "", 6,
239	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pgport)},
240
241	{"client_encoding", "PGCLIENTENCODING", NULL, NULL,
242		"Client-Encoding", "", 10,
243	offsetof(struct pg_conn, client_encoding_initial)},
244
245	{"options", "PGOPTIONS", DefaultOption, NULL,
246		"Backend-Options", "", 40,
247	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pgoptions)},
248
249	{"application_name", "PGAPPNAME", NULL, NULL,
250		"Application-Name", "", 64,
251	offsetof(struct pg_conn, appname)},
252
253	{"fallback_application_name", NULL, NULL, NULL,
254		"Fallback-Application-Name", "", 64,
255	offsetof(struct pg_conn, fbappname)},
256
257	{"keepalives", NULL, NULL, NULL,
258		"TCP-Keepalives", "", 1,	/* should be just '0' or '1' */
259	offsetof(struct pg_conn, keepalives)},
260
261	{"keepalives_idle", NULL, NULL, NULL,
262		"TCP-Keepalives-Idle", "", 10,	/* strlen(INT32_MAX) == 10 */
263	offsetof(struct pg_conn, keepalives_idle)},
264
265	{"keepalives_interval", NULL, NULL, NULL,
266		"TCP-Keepalives-Interval", "", 10,	/* strlen(INT32_MAX) == 10 */
267	offsetof(struct pg_conn, keepalives_interval)},
268
269	{"keepalives_count", NULL, NULL, NULL,
270		"TCP-Keepalives-Count", "", 10, /* strlen(INT32_MAX) == 10 */
271	offsetof(struct pg_conn, keepalives_count)},
272
273	{"tcp_user_timeout", NULL, NULL, NULL,
274		"TCP-User-Timeout", "", 10, /* strlen(INT32_MAX) == 10 */
275	offsetof(struct pg_conn, pgtcp_user_timeout)},
276
277	/*
278	 * ssl options are allowed even without client SSL support because the
279	 * client can still handle SSL modes "disable" and "allow". Other
280	 * parameters have no effect on non-SSL connections, so there is no reason
281	 * to exclude them since none of them are mandatory.
282	 */
283	{"sslmode", "PGSSLMODE", DefaultSSLMode, NULL,
284		"SSL-Mode", "", 12,		/* sizeof("verify-full") == 12 */
285	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslmode)},
286
287	{"sslnegotiation", "PGSSLNEGOTIATION", DefaultSSLNegotiation, NULL,
288		"SSL-Negotiation", "", 9,	/* sizeof("postgres") == 9  */
289	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslnegotiation)},
290
291	{"sslcompression", "PGSSLCOMPRESSION", "0", NULL,
292		"SSL-Compression", "", 1,
293	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslcompression)},
294
295	{"sslcert", "PGSSLCERT", NULL, NULL,
296		"SSL-Client-Cert", "", 64,
297	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslcert)},
298
299	{"sslkey", "PGSSLKEY", NULL, NULL,
300		"SSL-Client-Key", "", 64,
301	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslkey)},
302
303	{"sslcertmode", "PGSSLCERTMODE", NULL, NULL,
304		"SSL-Client-Cert-Mode", "", 8,	/* sizeof("disable") == 8 */
305	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslcertmode)},
306
307	{"sslpassword", NULL, NULL, NULL,
308		"SSL-Client-Key-Password", "*", 20,
309	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslpassword)},
310
311	{"sslrootcert", "PGSSLROOTCERT", NULL, NULL,
312		"SSL-Root-Certificate", "", 64,
313	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslrootcert)},
314
315	{"sslcrl", "PGSSLCRL", NULL, NULL,
316		"SSL-Revocation-List", "", 64,
317	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslcrl)},
318
319	{"sslcrldir", "PGSSLCRLDIR", NULL, NULL,
320		"SSL-Revocation-List-Dir", "", 64,
321	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslcrldir)},
322
323	{"sslsni", "PGSSLSNI", "1", NULL,
324		"SSL-SNI", "", 1,
325	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslsni)},
326
327	{"requirepeer", "PGREQUIREPEER", NULL, NULL,
328		"Require-Peer", "", 10,
329	offsetof(struct pg_conn, requirepeer)},
330
331	{"require_auth", "PGREQUIREAUTH", NULL, NULL,
332		"Require-Auth", "", 14, /* sizeof("scram-sha-256") == 14 */
333	offsetof(struct pg_conn, require_auth)},
334
335	{"min_protocol_version", "PGMINPROTOCOLVERSION",
336		NULL, NULL,
337		"Min-Protocol-Version", "", 6,	/* sizeof("latest") = 6 */
338	offsetof(struct pg_conn, min_protocol_version)},
339
340	{"max_protocol_version", "PGMAXPROTOCOLVERSION",
341		NULL, NULL,
342		"Max-Protocol-Version", "", 6,	/* sizeof("latest") = 6 */
343	offsetof(struct pg_conn, max_protocol_version)},
344
345	{"ssl_min_protocol_version", "PGSSLMINPROTOCOLVERSION", "TLSv1.2", NULL,
346		"SSL-Minimum-Protocol-Version", "", 8,	/* sizeof("TLSv1.x") == 8 */
347	offsetof(struct pg_conn, ssl_min_protocol_version)},
348
349	{"ssl_max_protocol_version", "PGSSLMAXPROTOCOLVERSION", NULL, NULL,
350		"SSL-Maximum-Protocol-Version", "", 8,	/* sizeof("TLSv1.x") == 8 */
351	offsetof(struct pg_conn, ssl_max_protocol_version)},
352
353	/*
354	 * As with SSL, all GSS options are exposed even in builds that don't have
355	 * support.
356	 */
357	{"gssencmode", "PGGSSENCMODE", DefaultGSSMode, NULL,
358		"GSSENC-Mode", "", 8,	/* sizeof("disable") == 8 */
359	offsetof(struct pg_conn, gssencmode)},
360
361	/* Kerberos and GSSAPI authentication support specifying the service name */
362	{"krbsrvname", "PGKRBSRVNAME", PG_KRB_SRVNAM, NULL,
363		"Kerberos-service-name", "", 20,
364	offsetof(struct pg_conn, krbsrvname)},
365
366	{"gsslib", "PGGSSLIB", NULL, NULL,
367		"GSS-library", "", 7,	/* sizeof("gssapi") == 7 */
368	offsetof(struct pg_conn, gsslib)},
369
370	{"gssdelegation", "PGGSSDELEGATION", "0", NULL,
371		"GSS-delegation", "", 1,
372	offsetof(struct pg_conn, gssdelegation)},
373
374	{"replication", NULL, NULL, NULL,
375		"Replication", "D", 5,
376	offsetof(struct pg_conn, replication)},
377
378	{"target_session_attrs", "PGTARGETSESSIONATTRS",
379		DefaultTargetSessionAttrs, NULL,
380		"Target-Session-Attrs", "", 15, /* sizeof("prefer-standby") = 15 */
381	offsetof(struct pg_conn, target_session_attrs)},
382
383	{"load_balance_hosts", "PGLOADBALANCEHOSTS",
384		DefaultLoadBalanceHosts, NULL,
385		"Load-Balance-Hosts", "", 8,	/* sizeof("disable") = 8 */
386	offsetof(struct pg_conn, load_balance_hosts)},
387
388	{"scram_client_key", NULL, NULL, NULL, "SCRAM-Client-Key", "D", SCRAM_MAX_KEY_LEN * 2,
389	offsetof(struct pg_conn, scram_client_key)},
390
391	{"scram_server_key", NULL, NULL, NULL, "SCRAM-Server-Key", "D", SCRAM_MAX_KEY_LEN * 2,
392	offsetof(struct pg_conn, scram_server_key)},
393
394	/* OAuth v2 */
395	{"oauth_issuer", NULL, NULL, NULL,
396		"OAuth-Issuer", "", 40,
397	offsetof(struct pg_conn, oauth_issuer)},
398
399	{"oauth_client_id", NULL, NULL, NULL,
400		"OAuth-Client-ID", "", 40,
401	offsetof(struct pg_conn, oauth_client_id)},
402
403	{"oauth_client_secret", NULL, NULL, NULL,
404		"OAuth-Client-Secret", "*", 40,
405	offsetof(struct pg_conn, oauth_client_secret)},
406
407	{"oauth_scope", NULL, NULL, NULL,
408		"OAuth-Scope", "", 15,
409	offsetof(struct pg_conn, oauth_scope)},
410
411	{"sslkeylogfile", NULL, NULL, NULL,
412		"SSL-Key-Log-File", "D", 64,
413	offsetof(struct pg_conn, sslkeylogfile)},
414
415	/* Terminating entry --- MUST BE LAST */
416	{NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,
417	NULL, NULL, 0}
418};
419

423	{
424		"PGDATESTYLE", "datestyle"
425	},
426	{
427		"PGTZ", "timezone"
428	},
429	/* internal performance-related settings */
430	{
431		"PGGEQO", "geqo"
432	},
433	{
434		NULL, NULL
435	}
436};

445/* The connection URI must start with either of the following designators: */
446static const char uri_designator[] = "postgresql://";
447static const char short_uri_designator[] = "postgres://";

453#endif

456static PGPing internal_ping(PGconn *conn);

458static bool fillPGconn(PGconn *conn, PQconninfoOption *connOptions);
459static void freePGconn(PGconn *conn);
460static void release_conn_addrinfo(PGconn *conn);
461static int	store_conn_addrinfo(PGconn *conn, struct addrinfo *addrlist);

472											  bool use_defaults, int expand_dbname);

480									  PQconninfoOption *connOptions,
481									  PQExpBuffer errorMessage);

502							  const char *username, const char *pgpassfile);
503static void pgpassfileWarning(PGconn *conn);

509
510/* global variable because fe-auth.c needs to access it */
511pgthreadlock_t pg_g_threadlock = default_threadlock;
512
513
514/*
515 *		pqDropConnection
516 *
517 * Close any physical connection to the server, and reset associated
518 * state inside the connection object.  We don't release state that
519 * would be needed to reconnect, though, nor local state that might still
520 * be useful later.
521 *
522 * We can always flush the output buffer, since there's no longer any hope
523 * of sending that data.  However, unprocessed input data might still be
524 * valuable, so the caller must tell us whether to flush that or not.
525 */
526void
527pqDropConnection(PGconn *conn, bool flushInput)
528{
529	/* Drop any SSL state */
530	pqsecure_close(conn);
531
532	/* Close the socket itself */
533	if (conn->sock != PGINVALID_SOCKET)
534		closesocket(conn->sock);
535	conn->sock = PGINVALID_SOCKET;
536
537	/* Optionally discard any unread data */
538	if (flushInput)
539		conn->inStart = conn->inCursor = conn->inEnd = 0;
540
541	/* Always discard any unsent data */
542	conn->outCount = 0;
543
544	/* Likewise, discard any pending pipelined commands */
545	pqFreeCommandQueue(conn->cmd_queue_head);
546	conn->cmd_queue_head = conn->cmd_queue_tail = NULL;
547	pqFreeCommandQueue(conn->cmd_queue_recycle);
548	conn->cmd_queue_recycle = NULL;
549
550	/* Free authentication/encryption state */
551	if (conn->cleanup_async_auth)
552	{
553		/*
554		 * Any in-progress async authentication should be torn down first so
555		 * that cleanup_async_auth() can depend on the other authentication
556		 * state if necessary.
557		 */
558		conn->cleanup_async_auth(conn);
559		conn->cleanup_async_auth = NULL;
560	}
561	conn->async_auth = NULL;
562	/* cleanup_async_auth() should have done this, but make sure */
563	conn->altsock = PGINVALID_SOCKET;
564#ifdef ENABLE_GSS
565	{
566		OM_uint32	min_s;
567
568		if (conn->gcred != GSS_C_NO_CREDENTIAL)
569		{
570			gss_release_cred(&min_s, &conn->gcred);
571			conn->gcred = GSS_C_NO_CREDENTIAL;
572		}
573		if (conn->gctx)
574			gss_delete_sec_context(&min_s, &conn->gctx, GSS_C_NO_BUFFER);
575		if (conn->gtarg_nam)
576			gss_release_name(&min_s, &conn->gtarg_nam);
577		if (conn->gss_SendBuffer)
578		{
579			free(conn->gss_SendBuffer);
580			conn->gss_SendBuffer = NULL;
581		}
582		if (conn->gss_RecvBuffer)
583		{
584			free(conn->gss_RecvBuffer);
585			conn->gss_RecvBuffer = NULL;
586		}
587		if (conn->gss_ResultBuffer)
588		{
589			free(conn->gss_ResultBuffer);
590			conn->gss_ResultBuffer = NULL;
591		}
592		conn->gssenc = false;
593	}
594#endif
595#ifdef ENABLE_SSPI
596	if (conn->sspitarget)
597	{
598		free(conn->sspitarget);
599		conn->sspitarget = NULL;
600	}
601	if (conn->sspicred)
602	{
603		FreeCredentialsHandle(conn->sspicred);
604		free(conn->sspicred);
605		conn->sspicred = NULL;
606	}
607	if (conn->sspictx)
608	{
609		DeleteSecurityContext(conn->sspictx);
610		free(conn->sspictx);
611		conn->sspictx = NULL;
612	}
613	conn->usesspi = 0;
614#endif
615	if (conn->sasl_state)
616	{
617		conn->sasl->free(conn->sasl_state);
618		conn->sasl_state = NULL;
619	}
620}
621
622/*
623 * pqFreeCommandQueue
624 * Free all the entries of PGcmdQueueEntry queue passed.
625 */
626static void
627pqFreeCommandQueue(PGcmdQueueEntry *queue)
628{
629	while (queue != NULL)
630	{
631		PGcmdQueueEntry *cur = queue;
632
633		queue = cur->next;
634		free(cur->query);
635		free(cur);
636	}
637}
638
639/*
640 *		pqDropServerData
641 *
642 * Clear all connection state data that was received from (or deduced about)
643 * the server.  This is essential to do between connection attempts to
644 * different servers, else we may incorrectly hold over some data from the
645 * old server.
646 *
647 * It would be better to merge this into pqDropConnection, perhaps, but
648 * right now we cannot because that function is called immediately on
649 * detection of connection loss (cf. pqReadData, for instance).  This data
650 * should be kept until we are actually starting a new connection.
651 */
652static void
653pqDropServerData(PGconn *conn)
654{
655	PGnotify   *notify;
656	pgParameterStatus *pstatus;
657
658	/* Forget pending notifies */
659	notify = conn->notifyHead;
660	while (notify != NULL)
661	{
662		PGnotify   *prev = notify;
663
664		notify = notify->next;
665		free(prev);
666	}
667	conn->notifyHead = conn->notifyTail = NULL;
668
669	/* Reset ParameterStatus data, as well as variables deduced from it */
670	pstatus = conn->pstatus;
671	while (pstatus != NULL)
672	{
673		pgParameterStatus *prev = pstatus;
674
675		pstatus = pstatus->next;
676		free(prev);
677	}
678	conn->pstatus = NULL;
679	conn->client_encoding = PG_SQL_ASCII;
680	conn->std_strings = false;
681	conn->default_transaction_read_only = PG_BOOL_UNKNOWN;
682	conn->in_hot_standby = PG_BOOL_UNKNOWN;
683	conn->scram_sha_256_iterations = SCRAM_SHA_256_DEFAULT_ITERATIONS;
684	conn->sversion = 0;
685
686	/* Drop large-object lookup data */
687	free(conn->lobjfuncs);
688	conn->lobjfuncs = NULL;
689
690	/* Reset assorted other per-connection state */
691	conn->last_sqlstate[0] = '\0';
692	conn->pversion_negotiated = false;
693	conn->auth_req_received = false;
694	conn->client_finished_auth = false;
695	conn->password_needed = false;
696	conn->gssapi_used = false;
697	conn->write_failed = false;
698	free(conn->write_err_msg);
699	conn->write_err_msg = NULL;
700	conn->oauth_want_retry = false;
701
702	/*
703	 * Cancel connections need to retain their be_pid and be_cancel_key across
704	 * PQcancelReset invocations, otherwise they would not have access to the
705	 * secret token of the connection they are supposed to cancel.
706	 */
707	if (!conn->cancelRequest)
708	{
709		conn->be_pid = 0;
710		if (conn->be_cancel_key != NULL)
711		{
712			free(conn->be_cancel_key);
713			conn->be_cancel_key = NULL;
714		}
715		conn->be_cancel_key_len = 0;
716	}
717}
718
719
720/*
721 *		Connecting to a Database
722 *
723 * There are now six different ways a user of this API can connect to the
724 * database.  Two are not recommended for use in new code, because of their
725 * lack of extensibility with respect to the passing of options to the
726 * backend.  These are PQsetdb and PQsetdbLogin (the former now being a macro
727 * to the latter).
728 *
729 * If it is desired to connect in a synchronous (blocking) manner, use the
730 * function PQconnectdb or PQconnectdbParams. The former accepts a string of
731 * option = value pairs (or a URI) which must be parsed; the latter takes two
732 * NULL terminated arrays instead.
733 *
734 * To connect in an asynchronous (non-blocking) manner, use the functions
735 * PQconnectStart or PQconnectStartParams (which differ in the same way as
736 * PQconnectdb and PQconnectdbParams) and PQconnectPoll.
737 *
738 * The non-exported functions pqConnectDBStart, pqConnectDBComplete are
739 * part of the connection procedure implementation.
740 */
741
742/*
743 *		PQconnectdbParams
744 *
745 * establishes a connection to a postgres backend through the postmaster
746 * using connection information in two arrays.
747 *
748 * The keywords array is defined as
749 *
750 *	   const char *params[] = {"option1", "option2", NULL}
751 *
752 * The values array is defined as
753 *
754 *	   const char *values[] = {"value1", "value2", NULL}
755 *
756 * Returns a PGconn* which is needed for all subsequent libpq calls, or NULL
757 * if a memory allocation failed.
758 * If the status field of the connection returned is CONNECTION_BAD,
759 * then some fields may be null'ed out instead of having valid values.
760 *
761 * You should call PQfinish (if conn is not NULL) regardless of whether this
762 * call succeeded.
763 */
764PGconn *
765PQconnectdbParams(const char *const *keywords,
766				  const char *const *values,
767				  int expand_dbname)
768{
769	PGconn	   *conn = PQconnectStartParams(keywords, values, expand_dbname);
770
771	if (conn && conn->status != CONNECTION_BAD)
772		(void) pqConnectDBComplete(conn);
773
774	return conn;
775}
776
777/*
778 *		PQpingParams
779 *
780 * check server status, accepting parameters identical to PQconnectdbParams
781 */
782PGPing
783PQpingParams(const char *const *keywords,
784			 const char *const *values,
785			 int expand_dbname)
786{
787	PGconn	   *conn = PQconnectStartParams(keywords, values, expand_dbname);
788	PGPing		ret;
789
790	ret = internal_ping(conn);
791	PQfinish(conn);
792
793	return ret;
794}
795
796/*
797 *		PQconnectdb
798 *
799 * establishes a connection to a postgres backend through the postmaster
800 * using connection information in a string.
801 *
802 * The conninfo string is either a whitespace-separated list of
803 *
804 *	   option = value
805 *
806 * definitions or a URI (refer to the documentation for details.) Value
807 * might be a single value containing no whitespaces or a single quoted
808 * string. If a single quote should appear anywhere in the value, it must be
809 * escaped with a backslash like \'
810 *
811 * Returns a PGconn* which is needed for all subsequent libpq calls, or NULL
812 * if a memory allocation failed.
813 * If the status field of the connection returned is CONNECTION_BAD,
814 * then some fields may be null'ed out instead of having valid values.
815 *
816 * You should call PQfinish (if conn is not NULL) regardless of whether this
817 * call succeeded.
818 */
819PGconn *
820PQconnectdb(const char *conninfo)
821{
822	PGconn	   *conn = PQconnectStart(conninfo);
823
824	if (conn && conn->status != CONNECTION_BAD)
825		(void) pqConnectDBComplete(conn);
826
827	return conn;
828}
829
830/*
831 *		PQping
832 *
833 * check server status, accepting parameters identical to PQconnectdb
834 */
835PGPing
836PQping(const char *conninfo)
837{
838	PGconn	   *conn = PQconnectStart(conninfo);
839	PGPing		ret;
840
841	ret = internal_ping(conn);
842	PQfinish(conn);
843
844	return ret;
845}
846
847/*
848 *		PQconnectStartParams
849 *
850 * Begins the establishment of a connection to a postgres backend through the
851 * postmaster using connection information in a struct.
852 *
853 * See comment for PQconnectdbParams for the definition of the string format.
854 *
855 * Returns a PGconn*.  If NULL is returned, a malloc error has occurred, and
856 * you should not attempt to proceed with this connection.  If the status
857 * field of the connection returned is CONNECTION_BAD, an error has
858 * occurred. In this case you should call PQfinish on the result, (perhaps
859 * inspecting the error message first).  Other fields of the structure may not
860 * be valid if that occurs.  If the status field is not CONNECTION_BAD, then
861 * this stage has succeeded - call PQconnectPoll, using select(2) to see when
862 * this is necessary.
863 *
864 * See PQconnectPoll for more info.
865 */
866PGconn *
867PQconnectStartParams(const char *const *keywords,
868					 const char *const *values,
869					 int expand_dbname)
870{
871	PGconn	   *conn;
872	PQconninfoOption *connOptions;
873
874	/*
875	 * Allocate memory for the conn structure.  Note that we also expect this
876	 * to initialize conn->errorMessage to empty.  All subsequent steps during
877	 * connection initialization will only append to that buffer.
878	 */
879	conn = pqMakeEmptyPGconn();
880	if (conn == NULL)
881		return NULL;
882
883	/*
884	 * Parse the conninfo arrays
885	 */
886	connOptions = conninfo_array_parse(keywords, values,
887									   &conn->errorMessage,
888									   true, expand_dbname);
889	if (connOptions == NULL)
890	{
891		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
892		/* errorMessage is already set */
893		return conn;
894	}
895
896	/*
897	 * Move option values into conn structure
898	 */
899	if (!fillPGconn(conn, connOptions))
900	{
901		PQconninfoFree(connOptions);
902		return conn;
903	}
904
905	/*
906	 * Free the option info - all is in conn now
907	 */
908	PQconninfoFree(connOptions);
909
910	/*
911	 * Compute derived options
912	 */
913	if (!pqConnectOptions2(conn))
914		return conn;
915
916	/*
917	 * Connect to the database
918	 */
919	if (!pqConnectDBStart(conn))
920	{
921		/* Just in case we failed to set it in pqConnectDBStart */
922		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
923	}
924
925	return conn;
926}
927
928/*
929 *		PQconnectStart
930 *
931 * Begins the establishment of a connection to a postgres backend through the
932 * postmaster using connection information in a string.
933 *
934 * See comment for PQconnectdb for the definition of the string format.
935 *
936 * Returns a PGconn*.  If NULL is returned, a malloc error has occurred, and
937 * you should not attempt to proceed with this connection.  If the status
938 * field of the connection returned is CONNECTION_BAD, an error has
939 * occurred. In this case you should call PQfinish on the result, (perhaps
940 * inspecting the error message first).  Other fields of the structure may not
941 * be valid if that occurs.  If the status field is not CONNECTION_BAD, then
942 * this stage has succeeded - call PQconnectPoll, using select(2) to see when
943 * this is necessary.
944 *
945 * See PQconnectPoll for more info.
946 */
947PGconn *
948PQconnectStart(const char *conninfo)
949{
950	PGconn	   *conn;
951
952	/*
953	 * Allocate memory for the conn structure.  Note that we also expect this
954	 * to initialize conn->errorMessage to empty.  All subsequent steps during
955	 * connection initialization will only append to that buffer.
956	 */
957	conn = pqMakeEmptyPGconn();
958	if (conn == NULL)
959		return NULL;
960
961	/*
962	 * Parse the conninfo string
963	 */
964	if (!connectOptions1(conn, conninfo))
965		return conn;
966
967	/*
968	 * Compute derived options
969	 */
970	if (!pqConnectOptions2(conn))
971		return conn;
972
973	/*
974	 * Connect to the database
975	 */
976	if (!pqConnectDBStart(conn))
977	{
978		/* Just in case we failed to set it in pqConnectDBStart */
979		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
980	}
981
982	return conn;
983}
984
985/*
986 * Move option values into conn structure
987 *
988 * Don't put anything cute here --- intelligence should be in
989 * pqConnectOptions2 ...
990 *
991 * Returns true on success. On failure, returns false and sets error message.
992 */
993static bool
994fillPGconn(PGconn *conn, PQconninfoOption *connOptions)
995{
996	const internalPQconninfoOption *option;
997
998	for (option = PQconninfoOptions; option->keyword; option++)
999	{
1000		if (option->connofs >= 0)
1001		{
1002			const char *tmp = conninfo_getval(connOptions, option->keyword);
1003
1004			if (tmp)
1005			{
1006				char	  **connmember = (char **) ((char *) conn + option->connofs);
1007
1008				free(*connmember);
1009				*connmember = strdup(tmp);
1010				if (*connmember == NULL)
1011				{
1012					libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "out of memory");
1013					return false;
1014				}
1015			}
1016		}
1017	}
1018
1019	return true;
1020}
1021
1022/*
1023 * Copy over option values from srcConn to dstConn
1024 *
1025 * Don't put anything cute here --- intelligence should be in
1026 * pqConnectOptions2 ...
1027 *
1028 * Returns true on success. On failure, returns false and sets error message of
1029 * dstConn.
1030 */
1031bool
1032pqCopyPGconn(PGconn *srcConn, PGconn *dstConn)
1033{
1034	const internalPQconninfoOption *option;
1035
1036	/* copy over connection options */
1037	for (option = PQconninfoOptions; option->keyword; option++)
1038	{
1039		if (option->connofs >= 0)
1040		{
1041			const char **tmp = (const char **) ((char *) srcConn + option->connofs);
1042
1043			if (*tmp)
1044			{
1045				char	  **dstConnmember = (char **) ((char *) dstConn + option->connofs);
1046
1047				if (*dstConnmember)
1048					free(*dstConnmember);
1049				*dstConnmember = strdup(*tmp);
1050				if (*dstConnmember == NULL)
1051				{
1052					libpq_append_conn_error(dstConn, "out of memory");
1053					return false;
1054				}
1055			}
1056		}
1057	}
1058	return true;
1059}
1060
1061/*
1062 *		connectOptions1
1063 *
1064 * Internal subroutine to set up connection parameters given an already-
1065 * created PGconn and a conninfo string.  Derived settings should be
1066 * processed by calling pqConnectOptions2 next.  (We split them because
1067 * PQsetdbLogin overrides defaults in between.)
1068 *
1069 * Returns true if OK, false if trouble (in which case errorMessage is set
1070 * and so is conn->status).
1071 */
1072static bool
1073connectOptions1(PGconn *conn, const char *conninfo)
1074{
1075	PQconninfoOption *connOptions;
1076
1077	/*
1078	 * Parse the conninfo string
1079	 */
1080	connOptions = parse_connection_string(conninfo, &conn->errorMessage, true);
1081	if (connOptions == NULL)
1082	{
1083		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1084		/* errorMessage is already set */
1085		return false;
1086	}
1087
1088	/*
1089	 * Move option values into conn structure
1090	 */
1091	if (!fillPGconn(conn, connOptions))
1092	{
1093		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1094		PQconninfoFree(connOptions);
1095		return false;
1096	}
1097
1098	/*
1099	 * Free the option info - all is in conn now
1100	 */
1101	PQconninfoFree(connOptions);
1102
1103	return true;
1104}
1105
1106/*
1107 * Count the number of elements in a simple comma-separated list.
1108 */
1109static int
1110count_comma_separated_elems(const char *input)
1111{
1112	int			n;
1113
1114	n = 1;
1115	for (; *input != '\0'; input++)
1116	{
1117		if (*input == ',')
1118			n++;
1119	}
1120
1121	return n;
1122}
1123
1124/*
1125 * Parse a simple comma-separated list.
1126 *
1127 * On each call, returns a malloc'd copy of the next element, and sets *more
1128 * to indicate whether there are any more elements in the list after this,
1129 * and updates *startptr to point to the next element, if any.
1130 *
1131 * On out of memory, returns NULL.
1132 */
1133static char *
1134parse_comma_separated_list(char **startptr, bool *more)
1135{
1136	char	   *p;
1137	char	   *s = *startptr;
1138	char	   *e;
1139	size_t		len;
1140
1141	/*
1142	 * Search for the end of the current element; a comma or end-of-string
1143	 * acts as a terminator.
1144	 */
1145	e = s;
1146	while (*e != '\0' && *e != ',')
1147		++e;
1148	*more = (*e == ',');
1149
1150	len = e - s;
1151	p = (char *) malloc(sizeof(char) * (len + 1));
1152	if (p)
1153	{
1154		memcpy(p, s, len);
1155		p[len] = '\0';
1156	}
1157	*startptr = e + 1;
1158
1159	return p;
1160}
1161
1162/*
1163 * Initializes the prng_state field of the connection. We want something
1164 * unpredictable, so if possible, use high-quality random bits for the
1165 * seed. Otherwise, fall back to a seed based on the connection address,
1166 * timestamp and PID.
1167 */
1168static void
1169libpq_prng_init(PGconn *conn)
1170{
1171	uint64		rseed;
1172	struct timeval tval = {0};
1173
1174	if (pg_prng_strong_seed(&conn->prng_state))
1175		return;
1176
1177	gettimeofday(&tval, NULL);
1178
1179	rseed = ((uintptr_t) conn) ^
1180		((uint64) getpid()) ^
1181		((uint64) tval.tv_usec) ^
1182		((uint64) tval.tv_sec);
1183
1184	pg_prng_seed(&conn->prng_state, rseed);
1185}
1186
1187/*
1188 * Fills the connection's allowed_sasl_mechs list with all supported SASL
1189 * mechanisms.
1190 */
1191static inline void
1192fill_allowed_sasl_mechs(PGconn *conn)
1193{
1194	/*---
1195	 * We only support two mechanisms at the moment, so rather than deal with a
1196	 * linked list, conn->allowed_sasl_mechs is an array of static length. We
1197	 * rely on the compile-time assertion here to keep us honest.
1198	 *
1199	 * To add a new mechanism to require_auth,
1200	 * - add it to supported_sasl_mechs,
1201	 * - update the length of conn->allowed_sasl_mechs,
1202	 * - handle the new mechanism name in the require_auth portion of
1203	 *   pqConnectOptions2(), below.
1204	 */
1205	StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(conn->allowed_sasl_mechs) == SASL_MECHANISM_COUNT,
1206					 "conn->allowed_sasl_mechs[] is not sufficiently large for holding all supported SASL mechanisms");
1207
1208	for (int i = 0; i < SASL_MECHANISM_COUNT; i++)
1209		conn->allowed_sasl_mechs[i] = supported_sasl_mechs[i];
1210}
1211
1212/*
1213 * Clears the connection's allowed_sasl_mechs list.
1214 */
1215static inline void
1216clear_allowed_sasl_mechs(PGconn *conn)
1217{
1218	for (int i = 0; i < lengthof(conn->allowed_sasl_mechs); i++)
1219		conn->allowed_sasl_mechs[i] = NULL;
1220}
1221
1222/*
1223 * Helper routine that searches the static allowed_sasl_mechs list for a
1224 * specific mechanism.
1225 */
1226static inline int
1227index_of_allowed_sasl_mech(PGconn *conn, const pg_fe_sasl_mech *mech)
1228{
1229	for (int i = 0; i < lengthof(conn->allowed_sasl_mechs); i++)
1230	{
1231		if (conn->allowed_sasl_mechs[i] == mech)
1232			return i;
1233	}
1234
1235	return -1;
1236}
1237
1238/*
1239 *		pqConnectOptions2
1240 *
1241 * Compute derived connection options after absorbing all user-supplied info.
1242 *
1243 * Returns true if OK, false if trouble (in which case errorMessage is set
1244 * and so is conn->status).
1245 */
1246bool
1247pqConnectOptions2(PGconn *conn)
1248{
1249	int			i;
1250
1251	/*
1252	 * Allocate memory for details about each host to which we might possibly
1253	 * try to connect.  For that, count the number of elements in the hostaddr
1254	 * or host options.  If neither is given, assume one host.
1255	 */
1256	conn->whichhost = 0;
1257	if (conn->pghostaddr && conn->pghostaddr[0] != '\0')
1258		conn->nconnhost = count_comma_separated_elems(conn->pghostaddr);
1259	else if (conn->pghost && conn->pghost[0] != '\0')
1260		conn->nconnhost = count_comma_separated_elems(conn->pghost);
1261	else
1262		conn->nconnhost = 1;
1263	conn->connhost = (pg_conn_host *)
1264		calloc(conn->nconnhost, sizeof(pg_conn_host));
1265	if (conn->connhost == NULL)
1266		goto oom_error;
1267
1268	/*
1269	 * We now have one pg_conn_host structure per possible host.  Fill in the
1270	 * host and hostaddr fields for each, by splitting the parameter strings.
1271	 */
1272	if (conn->pghostaddr != NULL && conn->pghostaddr[0] != '\0')
1273	{
1274		char	   *s = conn->pghostaddr;
1275		bool		more = true;
1276
1277		for (i = 0; i < conn->nconnhost && more; i++)
1278		{
1279			conn->connhost[i].hostaddr = parse_comma_separated_list(&s, &more);
1280			if (conn->connhost[i].hostaddr == NULL)
1281				goto oom_error;
1282		}
1283
1284		/*
1285		 * If hostaddr was given, the array was allocated according to the
1286		 * number of elements in the hostaddr list, so it really should be the
1287		 * right size.
1288		 */
1289		Assert(!more);
1290		Assert(i == conn->nconnhost);
1291	}
1292
1293	if (conn->pghost != NULL && conn->pghost[0] != '\0')
1294	{
1295		char	   *s = conn->pghost;
1296		bool		more = true;
1297
1298		for (i = 0; i < conn->nconnhost && more; i++)
1299		{
1300			conn->connhost[i].host = parse_comma_separated_list(&s, &more);
1301			if (conn->connhost[i].host == NULL)
1302				goto oom_error;
1303		}
1304
1305		/* Check for wrong number of host items. */
1306		if (more || i != conn->nconnhost)
1307		{
1308			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1309			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "could not match %d host names to %d hostaddr values",
1310									count_comma_separated_elems(conn->pghost), conn->nconnhost);
1311			return false;
1312		}
1313	}
1314
1315	/*
1316	 * Now, for each host slot, identify the type of address spec, and fill in
1317	 * the default address if nothing was given.
1318	 */
1319	for (i = 0; i < conn->nconnhost; i++)
1320	{
1321		pg_conn_host *ch = &conn->connhost[i];
1322
1323		if (ch->hostaddr != NULL && ch->hostaddr[0] != '\0')
1324			ch->type = CHT_HOST_ADDRESS;
1325		else if (ch->host != NULL && ch->host[0] != '\0')
1326		{
1327			ch->type = CHT_HOST_NAME;
1328			if (is_unixsock_path(ch->host))
1329				ch->type = CHT_UNIX_SOCKET;
1330		}
1331		else
1332		{
1333			free(ch->host);
1334
1335			/*
1336			 * This bit selects the default host location.  If you change
1337			 * this, see also pg_regress.
1338			 */
1339			if (DEFAULT_PGSOCKET_DIR[0])
1340			{
1341				ch->host = strdup(DEFAULT_PGSOCKET_DIR);
1342				ch->type = CHT_UNIX_SOCKET;
1343			}
1344			else
1345			{
1346				ch->host = strdup(DefaultHost);
1347				ch->type = CHT_HOST_NAME;
1348			}
1349			if (ch->host == NULL)
1350				goto oom_error;
1351		}
1352	}
1353
1354	/*
1355	 * Next, work out the port number corresponding to each host name.
1356	 *
1357	 * Note: unlike the above for host names, this could leave the port fields
1358	 * as null or empty strings.  We will substitute DEF_PGPORT whenever we
1359	 * read such a port field.
1360	 */
1361	if (conn->pgport != NULL && conn->pgport[0] != '\0')
1362	{
1363		char	   *s = conn->pgport;
1364		bool		more = true;
1365
1366		for (i = 0; i < conn->nconnhost && more; i++)
1367		{
1368			conn->connhost[i].port = parse_comma_separated_list(&s, &more);
1369			if (conn->connhost[i].port == NULL)
1370				goto oom_error;
1371		}
1372
1373		/*
1374		 * If exactly one port was given, use it for every host.  Otherwise,
1375		 * there must be exactly as many ports as there were hosts.
1376		 */
1377		if (i == 1 && !more)
1378		{
1379			for (i = 1; i < conn->nconnhost; i++)
1380			{
1381				conn->connhost[i].port = strdup(conn->connhost[0].port);
1382				if (conn->connhost[i].port == NULL)
1383					goto oom_error;
1384			}
1385		}
1386		else if (more || i != conn->nconnhost)
1387		{
1388			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1389			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "could not match %d port numbers to %d hosts",
1390									count_comma_separated_elems(conn->pgport), conn->nconnhost);
1391			return false;
1392		}
1393	}
1394
1395	/*
1396	 * If user name was not given, fetch it.  (Most likely, the fetch will
1397	 * fail, since the only way we get here is if pg_fe_getauthname() failed
1398	 * during conninfo_add_defaults().  But now we want an error message.)
1399	 */
1400	if (conn->pguser == NULL || conn->pguser[0] == '\0')
1401	{
1402		free(conn->pguser);
1403		conn->pguser = pg_fe_getauthname(&conn->errorMessage);
1404		if (!conn->pguser)
1405		{
1406			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1407			return false;
1408		}
1409	}
1410
1411	/*
1412	 * If database name was not given, default it to equal user name
1413	 */
1414	if (conn->dbName == NULL || conn->dbName[0] == '\0')
1415	{
1416		free(conn->dbName);
1417		conn->dbName = strdup(conn->pguser);
1418		if (!conn->dbName)
1419			goto oom_error;
1420	}
1421
1422	/*
1423	 * If password was not given, try to look it up in password file.  Note
1424	 * that the result might be different for each host/port pair.
1425	 */
1426	if (conn->pgpass == NULL || conn->pgpass[0] == '\0')
1427	{
1428		/* If password file wasn't specified, use ~/PGPASSFILE */
1429		if (conn->pgpassfile == NULL || conn->pgpassfile[0] == '\0')
1430		{
1431			char		homedir[MAXPGPATH];
1432
1433			if (pqGetHomeDirectory(homedir, sizeof(homedir)))
1434			{
1435				free(conn->pgpassfile);
1436				conn->pgpassfile = malloc(MAXPGPATH);
1437				if (!conn->pgpassfile)
1438					goto oom_error;
1439				snprintf(conn->pgpassfile, MAXPGPATH, "%s/%s",
1440						 homedir, PGPASSFILE);
1441			}
1442		}
1443
1444		if (conn->pgpassfile != NULL && conn->pgpassfile[0] != '\0')
1445		{
1446			for (i = 0; i < conn->nconnhost; i++)
1447			{
1448				/*
1449				 * Try to get a password for this host from file.  We use host
1450				 * for the hostname search key if given, else hostaddr (at
1451				 * least one of them is guaranteed nonempty by now).
1452				 */
1453				const char *pwhost = conn->connhost[i].host;
1454
1455				if (pwhost == NULL || pwhost[0] == '\0')
1456					pwhost = conn->connhost[i].hostaddr;
1457
1458				conn->connhost[i].password =
1459					passwordFromFile(pwhost,
1460									 conn->connhost[i].port,
1461									 conn->dbName,
1462									 conn->pguser,
1463									 conn->pgpassfile);
1464			}
1465		}
1466	}
1467
1468	/*
1469	 * parse and validate require_auth option
1470	 */
1471	if (conn->require_auth && conn->require_auth[0])
1472	{
1473		char	   *s = conn->require_auth;
1474		bool		first,
1475					more;
1476		bool		negated = false;
1477
1478		/*
1479		 * By default, start from an empty set of allowed methods and
1480		 * mechanisms, and add to it.
1481		 */
1482		conn->auth_required = true;
1483		conn->allowed_auth_methods = 0;
1484		clear_allowed_sasl_mechs(conn);
1485
1486		for (first = true, more = true; more; first = false)
1487		{
1488			char	   *method,
1489					   *part;
1490			uint32		bits = 0;
1491			const pg_fe_sasl_mech *mech = NULL;
1492
1493			part = parse_comma_separated_list(&s, &more);
1494			if (part == NULL)
1495				goto oom_error;
1496
1497			/*
1498			 * Check for negation, e.g. '!password'. If one element is
1499			 * negated, they all have to be.
1500			 */
1501			method = part;
1502			if (*method == '!')
1503			{
1504				if (first)
1505				{
1506					/*
1507					 * Switch to a permissive set of allowed methods and
1508					 * mechanisms, and subtract from it.
1509					 */
1510					conn->auth_required = false;
1511					conn->allowed_auth_methods = -1;
1512					fill_allowed_sasl_mechs(conn);
1513				}
1514				else if (!negated)
1515				{
1516					conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1517					libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "negative require_auth method \"%s\" cannot be mixed with non-negative methods",
1518											method);
1519
1520					free(part);
1521					return false;
1522				}
1523
1524				negated = true;
1525				method++;
1526			}
1527			else if (negated)
1528			{
1529				conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1530				libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "require_auth method \"%s\" cannot be mixed with negative methods",
1531										method);
1532
1533				free(part);
1534				return false;
1535			}
1536
1537			/*
1538			 * First group: methods that can be handled solely with the
1539			 * authentication request codes.
1540			 */
1541			if (strcmp(method, "password") == 0)
1542			{
1543				bits = (1 << AUTH_REQ_PASSWORD);
1544			}
1545			else if (strcmp(method, "md5") == 0)
1546			{
1547				bits = (1 << AUTH_REQ_MD5);
1548			}
1549			else if (strcmp(method, "gss") == 0)
1550			{
1551				bits = (1 << AUTH_REQ_GSS);
1552				bits |= (1 << AUTH_REQ_GSS_CONT);
1553			}
1554			else if (strcmp(method, "sspi") == 0)
1555			{
1556				bits = (1 << AUTH_REQ_SSPI);
1557				bits |= (1 << AUTH_REQ_GSS_CONT);
1558			}
1559
1560			/*
1561			 * Next group: SASL mechanisms. All of these use the same request
1562			 * codes, so the list of allowed mechanisms is tracked separately.
1563			 *
1564			 * supported_sasl_mechs must contain all mechanisms handled here.
1565			 */
1566			else if (strcmp(method, "scram-sha-256") == 0)
1567			{
1568				mech = &pg_scram_mech;
1569			}
1570			else if (strcmp(method, "oauth") == 0)
1571			{
1572				mech = &pg_oauth_mech;
1573			}
1574
1575			/*
1576			 * Final group: meta-options.
1577			 */
1578			else if (strcmp(method, "none") == 0)
1579			{
1580				/*
1581				 * Special case: let the user explicitly allow (or disallow)
1582				 * connections where the server does not send an explicit
1583				 * authentication challenge, such as "trust" and "cert" auth.
1584				 */
1585				if (negated)	/* "!none" */
1586				{
1587					if (conn->auth_required)
1588						goto duplicate;
1589
1590					conn->auth_required = true;
1591				}
1592				else			/* "none" */
1593				{
1594					if (!conn->auth_required)
1595						goto duplicate;
1596
1597					conn->auth_required = false;
1598				}
1599
1600				free(part);
1601				continue;		/* avoid the bitmask manipulation below */
1602			}
1603			else
1604			{
1605				conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1606				libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
1607										"require_auth", method);
1608
1609				free(part);
1610				return false;
1611			}
1612
1613			if (mech)
1614			{
1615				/*
1616				 * Update the mechanism set only. The method bitmask will be
1617				 * updated for SASL further down.
1618				 */
1619				Assert(!bits);
1620
1621				if (negated)
1622				{
1623					/* Remove the existing mechanism from the list. */
1624					i = index_of_allowed_sasl_mech(conn, mech);
1625					if (i < 0)
1626						goto duplicate;
1627
1628					conn->allowed_sasl_mechs[i] = NULL;
1629				}
1630				else
1631				{
1632					/*
1633					 * Find a space to put the new mechanism (after making
1634					 * sure it's not already there).
1635					 */
1636					i = index_of_allowed_sasl_mech(conn, mech);
1637					if (i >= 0)
1638						goto duplicate;
1639
1640					i = index_of_allowed_sasl_mech(conn, NULL);
1641					if (i < 0)
1642					{
1643						/* Should not happen; the pointer list is corrupted. */
1644						Assert(false);
1645
1646						conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1647						libpq_append_conn_error(conn,
1648												"internal error: no space in allowed_sasl_mechs");
1649						free(part);
1650						return false;
1651					}
1652
1653					conn->allowed_sasl_mechs[i] = mech;
1654				}
1655			}
1656			else
1657			{
1658				/* Update the method bitmask. */
1659				Assert(bits);
1660
1661				if (negated)
1662				{
1663					if ((conn->allowed_auth_methods & bits) == 0)
1664						goto duplicate;
1665
1666					conn->allowed_auth_methods &= ~bits;
1667				}
1668				else
1669				{
1670					if ((conn->allowed_auth_methods & bits) == bits)
1671						goto duplicate;
1672
1673					conn->allowed_auth_methods |= bits;
1674				}
1675			}
1676
1677			free(part);
1678			continue;
1679
1680	duplicate:
1681
1682			/*
1683			 * A duplicated method probably indicates a typo in a setting
1684			 * where typos are extremely risky.
1685			 */
1686			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1687			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "require_auth method \"%s\" is specified more than once",
1688									part);
1689
1690			free(part);
1691			return false;
1692		}
1693
1694		/*
1695		 * Finally, allow SASL authentication requests if (and only if) we've
1696		 * allowed any mechanisms.
1697		 */
1698		{
1699			bool		allowed = false;
1700			const uint32 sasl_bits =
1701				(1 << AUTH_REQ_SASL)
1702				| (1 << AUTH_REQ_SASL_CONT)
1703				| (1 << AUTH_REQ_SASL_FIN);
1704
1705			for (i = 0; i < lengthof(conn->allowed_sasl_mechs); i++)
1706			{
1707				if (conn->allowed_sasl_mechs[i])
1708				{
1709					allowed = true;
1710					break;
1711				}
1712			}
1713
1714			/*
1715			 * For the standard case, add the SASL bits to the (default-empty)
1716			 * set if needed. For the negated case, remove them.
1717			 */
1718			if (!negated && allowed)
1719				conn->allowed_auth_methods |= sasl_bits;
1720			else if (negated && !allowed)
1721				conn->allowed_auth_methods &= ~sasl_bits;
1722		}
1723	}
1724
1725	/*
1726	 * validate channel_binding option
1727	 */
1728	if (conn->channel_binding)
1729	{
1730		if (strcmp(conn->channel_binding, "disable") != 0
1731			&& strcmp(conn->channel_binding, "prefer") != 0
1732			&& strcmp(conn->channel_binding, "require") != 0)
1733		{
1734			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1735			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
1736									"channel_binding", conn->channel_binding);
1737			return false;
1738		}
1739	}
1740	else
1741	{
1742		conn->channel_binding = strdup(DefaultChannelBinding);
1743		if (!conn->channel_binding)
1744			goto oom_error;
1745	}
1746
1747#ifndef USE_SSL
1748
1749	/*
1750	 * sslrootcert=system is not supported. Since setting this changes the
1751	 * default sslmode, check this _before_ we validate sslmode, to avoid
1752	 * confusing the user with errors for an option they may not have set.
1753	 */
1754	if (conn->sslrootcert
1755		&& strcmp(conn->sslrootcert, "system") == 0)
1756	{
1757		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1758		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s value \"%s\" invalid when SSL support is not compiled in",
1759								"sslrootcert", conn->sslrootcert);
1760		return false;
1761	}
1762#endif
1763
1764	/*
1765	 * validate sslmode option
1766	 */
1767	if (conn->sslmode)
1768	{
1769		if (strcmp(conn->sslmode, "disable") != 0
1770			&& strcmp(conn->sslmode, "allow") != 0
1771			&& strcmp(conn->sslmode, "prefer") != 0
1772			&& strcmp(conn->sslmode, "require") != 0
1773			&& strcmp(conn->sslmode, "verify-ca") != 0
1774			&& strcmp(conn->sslmode, "verify-full") != 0)
1775		{
1776			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1777			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
1778									"sslmode", conn->sslmode);
1779			return false;
1780		}
1781
1782#ifndef USE_SSL
1783		switch (conn->sslmode[0])
1784		{
1785			case 'a':			/* "allow" */
1786			case 'p':			/* "prefer" */
1787
1788				/*
1789				 * warn user that an SSL connection will never be negotiated
1790				 * since SSL was not compiled in?
1791				 */
1792				break;
1793
1794			case 'r':			/* "require" */
1795			case 'v':			/* "verify-ca" or "verify-full" */
1796				conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1797				libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s value \"%s\" invalid when SSL support is not compiled in",
1798										"sslmode", conn->sslmode);
1799				return false;
1800		}
1801#endif
1802	}
1803	else
1804	{
1805		conn->sslmode = strdup(DefaultSSLMode);
1806		if (!conn->sslmode)
1807			goto oom_error;
1808	}
1809
1810	/*
1811	 * validate sslnegotiation option, default is "postgres" for the postgres
1812	 * style negotiated connection with an extra round trip but more options.
1813	 */
1814	if (conn->sslnegotiation)
1815	{
1816		if (strcmp(conn->sslnegotiation, "postgres") != 0
1817			&& strcmp(conn->sslnegotiation, "direct") != 0)
1818		{
1819			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1820			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
1821									"sslnegotiation", conn->sslnegotiation);
1822			return false;
1823		}
1824
1825#ifndef USE_SSL
1826		if (conn->sslnegotiation[0] != 'p')
1827		{
1828			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1829			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s value \"%s\" invalid when SSL support is not compiled in",
1830									"sslnegotiation", conn->sslnegotiation);
1831			return false;
1832		}
1833#endif
1834
1835		/*
1836		 * Don't allow direct SSL negotiation with sslmode='prefer', because
1837		 * that poses a risk of unintentional fallback to plaintext connection
1838		 * when connecting to a pre-v17 server that does not support direct
1839		 * SSL connections. To keep things simple, don't allow it with
1840		 * sslmode='allow' or sslmode='disable' either. If a user goes through
1841		 * the trouble of setting sslnegotiation='direct', they probably
1842		 * intend to use SSL, and sslmode=disable or allow is probably a user
1843		 * mistake anyway.
1844		 */
1845		if (conn->sslnegotiation[0] == 'd' &&
1846			conn->sslmode[0] != 'r' && conn->sslmode[0] != 'v')
1847		{
1848			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1849			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "weak sslmode \"%s\" may not be used with sslnegotiation=direct (use \"require\", \"verify-ca\", or \"verify-full\")",
1850									conn->sslmode);
1851			return false;
1852		}
1853	}
1854	else
1855	{
1856		conn->sslnegotiation = strdup(DefaultSSLNegotiation);
1857		if (!conn->sslnegotiation)
1858			goto oom_error;
1859	}
1860
1861#ifdef USE_SSL
1862
1863	/*
1864	 * If sslrootcert=system, make sure our chosen sslmode is compatible.
1865	 */
1866	if (conn->sslrootcert
1867		&& strcmp(conn->sslrootcert, "system") == 0
1868		&& strcmp(conn->sslmode, "verify-full") != 0)
1869	{
1870		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1871		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "weak sslmode \"%s\" may not be used with sslrootcert=system (use \"verify-full\")",
1872								conn->sslmode);
1873		return false;
1874	}
1875#endif
1876
1877	/*
1878	 * Validate TLS protocol versions for ssl_min_protocol_version and
1879	 * ssl_max_protocol_version.
1880	 */
1881	if (!sslVerifyProtocolVersion(conn->ssl_min_protocol_version))
1882	{
1883		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1884		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid \"%s\" value: \"%s\"",
1885								"ssl_min_protocol_version",
1886								conn->ssl_min_protocol_version);
1887		return false;
1888	}
1889	if (!sslVerifyProtocolVersion(conn->ssl_max_protocol_version))
1890	{
1891		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1892		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid \"%s\" value: \"%s\"",
1893								"ssl_max_protocol_version",
1894								conn->ssl_max_protocol_version);
1895		return false;
1896	}
1897
1898	/*
1899	 * Check if the range of SSL protocols defined is correct.  This is done
1900	 * at this early step because this is independent of the SSL
1901	 * implementation used, and this avoids unnecessary cycles with an
1902	 * already-built SSL context when the connection is being established, as
1903	 * it would be doomed anyway.
1904	 */
1905	if (!sslVerifyProtocolRange(conn->ssl_min_protocol_version,
1906								conn->ssl_max_protocol_version))
1907	{
1908		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1909		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid SSL protocol version range");
1910		return false;
1911	}
1912
1913	/*
1914	 * validate sslcertmode option
1915	 */
1916	if (conn->sslcertmode)
1917	{
1918		if (strcmp(conn->sslcertmode, "disable") != 0 &&
1919			strcmp(conn->sslcertmode, "allow") != 0 &&
1920			strcmp(conn->sslcertmode, "require") != 0)
1921		{
1922			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1923			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
1924									"sslcertmode", conn->sslcertmode);
1925			return false;
1926		}
1927#ifndef USE_SSL
1928		if (strcmp(conn->sslcertmode, "require") == 0)
1929		{
1930			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1931			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s value \"%s\" invalid when SSL support is not compiled in",
1932									"sslcertmode", conn->sslcertmode);
1933			return false;
1934		}
1935#endif
1936#ifndef HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_CERT_CB
1937
1938		/*
1939		 * Without a certificate callback, the current implementation can't
1940		 * figure out if a certificate was actually requested, so "require" is
1941		 * useless.
1942		 */
1943		if (strcmp(conn->sslcertmode, "require") == 0)
1944		{
1945			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1946			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s value \"%s\" is not supported (check OpenSSL version)",
1947									"sslcertmode", conn->sslcertmode);
1948			return false;
1949		}
1950#endif
1951	}
1952	else
1953	{
1954		conn->sslcertmode = strdup(DefaultSSLCertMode);
1955		if (!conn->sslcertmode)
1956			goto oom_error;
1957	}
1958
1959	/*
1960	 * validate gssencmode option
1961	 */
1962	if (conn->gssencmode)
1963	{
1964		if (strcmp(conn->gssencmode, "disable") != 0 &&
1965			strcmp(conn->gssencmode, "prefer") != 0 &&
1966			strcmp(conn->gssencmode, "require") != 0)
1967		{
1968			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1969			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"", "gssencmode", conn->gssencmode);
1970			return false;
1971		}
1972#ifndef ENABLE_GSS
1973		if (strcmp(conn->gssencmode, "require") == 0)
1974		{
1975			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
1976			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "gssencmode value \"%s\" invalid when GSSAPI support is not compiled in",
1977									conn->gssencmode);
1978			return false;
1979		}
1980#endif
1981	}
1982	else
1983	{
1984		conn->gssencmode = strdup(DefaultGSSMode);
1985		if (!conn->gssencmode)
1986			goto oom_error;
1987	}
1988
1989	/*
1990	 * validate target_session_attrs option, and set target_server_type
1991	 */
1992	if (conn->target_session_attrs)
1993	{
1994		if (strcmp(conn->target_session_attrs, "any") == 0)
1995			conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_ANY;
1996		else if (strcmp(conn->target_session_attrs, "read-write") == 0)
1997			conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_READ_WRITE;
1998		else if (strcmp(conn->target_session_attrs, "read-only") == 0)
1999			conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_READ_ONLY;
2000		else if (strcmp(conn->target_session_attrs, "primary") == 0)
2001			conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_PRIMARY;
2002		else if (strcmp(conn->target_session_attrs, "standby") == 0)
2003			conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_STANDBY;
2004		else if (strcmp(conn->target_session_attrs, "prefer-standby") == 0)
2005			conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_PREFER_STANDBY;
2006		else
2007		{
2008			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2009			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
2010									"target_session_attrs",
2011									conn->target_session_attrs);
2012			return false;
2013		}
2014	}
2015	else
2016		conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_ANY;
2017
2018	if (conn->scram_client_key)
2019	{
2020		int			len;
2021
2022		len = pg_b64_dec_len(strlen(conn->scram_client_key));
2023		conn->scram_client_key_binary = malloc(len);
2024		if (!conn->scram_client_key_binary)
2025			goto oom_error;
2026		len = pg_b64_decode(conn->scram_client_key, strlen(conn->scram_client_key),
2027							conn->scram_client_key_binary, len);
2028		if (len < 0)
2029		{
2030			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid SCRAM client key");
2031			return false;
2032		}
2033		if (len != SCRAM_MAX_KEY_LEN)
2034		{
2035			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid SCRAM client key length: %d", len);
2036			return false;
2037		}
2038		conn->scram_client_key_len = len;
2039	}
2040
2041	if (conn->scram_server_key)
2042	{
2043		int			len;
2044
2045		len = pg_b64_dec_len(strlen(conn->scram_server_key));
2046		conn->scram_server_key_binary = malloc(len);
2047		if (!conn->scram_server_key_binary)
2048			goto oom_error;
2049		len = pg_b64_decode(conn->scram_server_key, strlen(conn->scram_server_key),
2050							conn->scram_server_key_binary, len);
2051		if (len < 0)
2052		{
2053			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid SCRAM server key");
2054			return false;
2055		}
2056		if (len != SCRAM_MAX_KEY_LEN)
2057		{
2058			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid SCRAM server key length: %d", len);
2059			return false;
2060		}
2061		conn->scram_server_key_len = len;
2062	}
2063
2064	/*
2065	 * validate load_balance_hosts option, and set load_balance_type
2066	 */
2067	if (conn->load_balance_hosts)
2068	{
2069		if (strcmp(conn->load_balance_hosts, "disable") == 0)
2070			conn->load_balance_type = LOAD_BALANCE_DISABLE;
2071		else if (strcmp(conn->load_balance_hosts, "random") == 0)
2072			conn->load_balance_type = LOAD_BALANCE_RANDOM;
2073		else
2074		{
2075			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2076			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
2077									"load_balance_hosts",
2078									conn->load_balance_hosts);
2079			return false;
2080		}
2081	}
2082	else
2083		conn->load_balance_type = LOAD_BALANCE_DISABLE;
2084
2085	if (conn->load_balance_type == LOAD_BALANCE_RANDOM)
2086	{
2087		libpq_prng_init(conn);
2088
2089		/*
2090		 * This is the "inside-out" variant of the Fisher-Yates shuffle
2091		 * algorithm. Notionally, we append each new value to the array and
2092		 * then swap it with a randomly-chosen array element (possibly
2093		 * including itself, else we fail to generate permutations with the
2094		 * last integer last).  The swap step can be optimized by combining it
2095		 * with the insertion.
2096		 */
2097		for (i = 1; i < conn->nconnhost; i++)
2098		{
2099			int			j = pg_prng_uint64_range(&conn->prng_state, 0, i);
2100			pg_conn_host temp = conn->connhost[j];
2101
2102			conn->connhost[j] = conn->connhost[i];
2103			conn->connhost[i] = temp;
2104		}
2105	}
2106
2107	if (conn->min_protocol_version)
2108	{
2109		if (!pqParseProtocolVersion(conn->min_protocol_version, &conn->min_pversion, conn, "min_protocol_version"))
2110		{
2111			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2112			return false;
2113		}
2114	}
2115	else
2116	{
2117		conn->min_pversion = PG_PROTOCOL_EARLIEST;
2118	}
2119
2120	if (conn->max_protocol_version)
2121	{
2122		if (!pqParseProtocolVersion(conn->max_protocol_version, &conn->max_pversion, conn, "max_protocol_version"))
2123		{
2124			conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2125			return false;
2126		}
2127	}
2128	else
2129	{
2130		/*
2131		 * To not break connecting to older servers/poolers that do not yet
2132		 * support NegotiateProtocolVersion, default to the 3.0 protocol at
2133		 * least for a while longer. Except when min_protocol_version is set
2134		 * to something larger, then we might as well default to the latest.
2135		 */
2136		if (conn->min_pversion > PG_PROTOCOL(3, 0))
2137			conn->max_pversion = PG_PROTOCOL_LATEST;
2138		else
2139			conn->max_pversion = PG_PROTOCOL(3, 0);
2140	}
2141
2142	if (conn->min_pversion > conn->max_pversion)
2143	{
2144		conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2145		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "\"%s\" is greater than \"%s\"", "min_protocol_version", "max_protocol_version");
2146		return false;
2147	}
2148
2149	/*
2150	 * Resolve special "auto" client_encoding from the locale
2151	 */
2152	if (conn->client_encoding_initial &&
2153		strcmp(conn->client_encoding_initial, "auto") == 0)
2154	{
2155		free(conn->client_encoding_initial);
2156		conn->client_encoding_initial = strdup(pg_encoding_to_char(pg_get_encoding_from_locale(NULL, true)));
2157		if (!conn->client_encoding_initial)
2158			goto oom_error;
2159	}
2160
2161	/*
2162	 * Only if we get this far is it appropriate to try to connect. (We need a
2163	 * state flag, rather than just the boolean result of this function, in
2164	 * case someone tries to PQreset() the PGconn.)
2165	 */
2166	conn->options_valid = true;
2167
2168	return true;
2169
2170oom_error:
2171	conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2172	libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "out of memory");
2173	return false;
2174}
2175
2176/*
2177 *		PQconndefaults
2178 *
2179 * Construct a default connection options array, which identifies all the
2180 * available options and shows any default values that are available from the
2181 * environment etc.  On error (eg out of memory), NULL is returned.
2182 *
2183 * Using this function, an application may determine all possible options
2184 * and their current default values.
2185 *
2186 * NOTE: as of PostgreSQL 7.0, the returned array is dynamically allocated
2187 * and should be freed when no longer needed via PQconninfoFree().  (In prior
2188 * versions, the returned array was static, but that's not thread-safe.)
2189 * Pre-7.0 applications that use this function will see a small memory leak
2190 * until they are updated to call PQconninfoFree.
2191 */
2192PQconninfoOption *
2193PQconndefaults(void)
2194{
2195	PQExpBufferData errorBuf;
2196	PQconninfoOption *connOptions;
2197
2198	/* We don't actually report any errors here, but callees want a buffer */
2199	initPQExpBuffer(&errorBuf);
2200	if (PQExpBufferDataBroken(errorBuf))
2201		return NULL;			/* out of memory already :-( */
2202
2203	connOptions = conninfo_init(&errorBuf);
2204	if (connOptions != NULL)
2205	{
2206		/* pass NULL errorBuf to ignore errors */
2207		if (!conninfo_add_defaults(connOptions, NULL))
2208		{
2209			PQconninfoFree(connOptions);
2210			connOptions = NULL;
2211		}
2212	}
2213
2214	termPQExpBuffer(&errorBuf);
2215	return connOptions;
2216}
2217
2218/* ----------------
2219 *		PQsetdbLogin
2220 *
2221 * establishes a connection to a postgres backend through the postmaster
2222 * at the specified host and port.
2223 *
2224 * returns a PGconn* which is needed for all subsequent libpq calls
2225 *
2226 * if the status field of the connection returned is CONNECTION_BAD,
2227 * then only the errorMessage is likely to be useful.
2228 * ----------------
2229 */
2230PGconn *
2231PQsetdbLogin(const char *pghost, const char *pgport, const char *pgoptions,
2232			 const char *pgtty, const char *dbName, const char *login,
2233			 const char *pwd)
2234{
2235	PGconn	   *conn;
2236
2237	/*
2238	 * Allocate memory for the conn structure.  Note that we also expect this
2239	 * to initialize conn->errorMessage to empty.  All subsequent steps during
2240	 * connection initialization will only append to that buffer.
2241	 */
2242	conn = pqMakeEmptyPGconn();
2243	if (conn == NULL)
2244		return NULL;
2245
2246	/*
2247	 * If the dbName parameter contains what looks like a connection string,
2248	 * parse it into conn struct using connectOptions1.
2249	 */
2250	if (dbName && recognized_connection_string(dbName))
2251	{
2252		if (!connectOptions1(conn, dbName))
2253			return conn;
2254	}
2255	else
2256	{
2257		/*
2258		 * Old-style path: first, parse an empty conninfo string in order to
2259		 * set up the same defaults that PQconnectdb() would use.
2260		 */
2261		if (!connectOptions1(conn, ""))
2262			return conn;
2263
2264		/* Insert dbName parameter value into struct */
2265		if (dbName && dbName[0] != '\0')
2266		{
2267			free(conn->dbName);
2268			conn->dbName = strdup(dbName);
2269			if (!conn->dbName)
2270				goto oom_error;
2271		}
2272	}
2273
2274	/*
2275	 * Insert remaining parameters into struct, overriding defaults (as well
2276	 * as any conflicting data from dbName taken as a conninfo).
2277	 */
2278	if (pghost && pghost[0] != '\0')
2279	{
2280		free(conn->pghost);
2281		conn->pghost = strdup(pghost);
2282		if (!conn->pghost)
2283			goto oom_error;
2284	}
2285
2286	if (pgport && pgport[0] != '\0')
2287	{
2288		free(conn->pgport);
2289		conn->pgport = strdup(pgport);
2290		if (!conn->pgport)
2291			goto oom_error;
2292	}
2293
2294	if (pgoptions && pgoptions[0] != '\0')
2295	{
2296		free(conn->pgoptions);
2297		conn->pgoptions = strdup(pgoptions);
2298		if (!conn->pgoptions)
2299			goto oom_error;
2300	}
2301
2302	if (login && login[0] != '\0')
2303	{
2304		free(conn->pguser);
2305		conn->pguser = strdup(login);
2306		if (!conn->pguser)
2307			goto oom_error;
2308	}
2309
2310	if (pwd && pwd[0] != '\0')
2311	{
2312		free(conn->pgpass);
2313		conn->pgpass = strdup(pwd);
2314		if (!conn->pgpass)
2315			goto oom_error;
2316	}
2317
2318	/*
2319	 * Compute derived options
2320	 */
2321	if (!pqConnectOptions2(conn))
2322		return conn;
2323
2324	/*
2325	 * Connect to the database
2326	 */
2327	if (pqConnectDBStart(conn))
2328		(void) pqConnectDBComplete(conn);
2329
2330	return conn;
2331
2332oom_error:
2333	conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2334	libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "out of memory");
2335	return conn;
2336}
2337
2338
2339/* ----------
2340 * connectNoDelay -
2341 * Sets the TCP_NODELAY socket option.
2342 * Returns 1 if successful, 0 if not.
2343 * ----------
2344 */
2345static int
2346connectNoDelay(PGconn *conn)
2347{
2348#ifdef	TCP_NODELAY
2349	int			on = 1;
2350
2351	if (setsockopt(conn->sock, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_NODELAY,
2352				   (char *) &on,
2353				   sizeof(on)) < 0)
2354	{
2355		char		sebuf[PG_STRERROR_R_BUFLEN];
2356
2357		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "could not set socket to TCP no delay mode: %s",
2358								SOCK_STRERROR(SOCK_ERRNO, sebuf, sizeof(sebuf)));
2359		return 0;
2360	}
2361#endif
2362
2363	return 1;
2364}
2365
2366/* ----------
2367 * Write currently connected IP address into host_addr (of len host_addr_len).
2368 * If unable to, set it to the empty string.
2369 * ----------
2370 */
2371static void
2372getHostaddr(PGconn *conn, char *host_addr, int host_addr_len)
2373{
2374	struct sockaddr_storage *addr = &conn->raddr.addr;
2375
2376	if (addr->ss_family == AF_INET)
2377	{
2378		if (pg_inet_net_ntop(AF_INET,
2379							 &((struct sockaddr_in *) addr)->sin_addr.s_addr,
2380							 32,
2381							 host_addr, host_addr_len) == NULL)
2382			host_addr[0] = '\0';
2383	}
2384	else if (addr->ss_family == AF_INET6)
2385	{
2386		if (pg_inet_net_ntop(AF_INET6,
2387							 &((struct sockaddr_in6 *) addr)->sin6_addr.s6_addr,
2388							 128,
2389							 host_addr, host_addr_len) == NULL)
2390			host_addr[0] = '\0';
2391	}
2392	else
2393		host_addr[0] = '\0';
2394}
2395
2396/*
2397 * emitHostIdentityInfo -
2398 * Speculatively append "connection to server so-and-so failed: " to
2399 * conn->errorMessage once we've identified the current connection target
2400 * address.  This ensures that any subsequent error message will be properly
2401 * attributed to the server we couldn't connect to.  conn->raddr must be
2402 * valid, and the result of getHostaddr() must be supplied.
2403 */
2404static void
2405emitHostIdentityInfo(PGconn *conn, const char *host_addr)
2406{
2407	if (conn->raddr.addr.ss_family == AF_UNIX)
2408	{
2409		char		service[NI_MAXHOST];
2410
2411		pg_getnameinfo_all(&conn->raddr.addr, conn->raddr.salen,
2412						   NULL, 0,
2413						   service, sizeof(service),
2414						   NI_NUMERICSERV);
2415		appendPQExpBuffer(&conn->errorMessage,
2416						  libpq_gettext("connection to server on socket \"%s\" failed: "),
2417						  service);
2418	}
2419	else
2420	{
2421		const char *displayed_host;
2422		const char *displayed_port;
2423
2424		/* To which host and port were we actually connecting? */
2425		if (conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].type == CHT_HOST_ADDRESS)
2426			displayed_host = conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].hostaddr;
2427		else
2428			displayed_host = conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].host;
2429		displayed_port = conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].port;
2430		if (displayed_port == NULL || displayed_port[0] == '\0')
2431			displayed_port = DEF_PGPORT_STR;
2432
2433		/*
2434		 * If the user did not supply an IP address using 'hostaddr', and
2435		 * 'host' was missing or does not match our lookup, display the
2436		 * looked-up IP address.
2437		 */
2438		if (conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].type != CHT_HOST_ADDRESS &&
2439			host_addr[0] &&
2440			strcmp(displayed_host, host_addr) != 0)
2441			appendPQExpBuffer(&conn->errorMessage,
2442							  libpq_gettext("connection to server at \"%s\" (%s), port %s failed: "),
2443							  displayed_host, host_addr,
2444							  displayed_port);
2445		else
2446			appendPQExpBuffer(&conn->errorMessage,
2447							  libpq_gettext("connection to server at \"%s\", port %s failed: "),
2448							  displayed_host,
2449							  displayed_port);
2450	}
2451}
2452
2453/* ----------
2454 * connectFailureMessage -
2455 * create a friendly error message on connection failure,
2456 * using the given errno value.  Use this for error cases that
2457 * imply that there's no server there.
2458 * ----------
2459 */
2460static void
2461connectFailureMessage(PGconn *conn, int errorno)
2462{
2463	char		sebuf[PG_STRERROR_R_BUFLEN];
2464
2465	appendPQExpBuffer(&conn->errorMessage,
2466					  "%s\n",
2467					  SOCK_STRERROR(errorno, sebuf, sizeof(sebuf)));
2468
2469	if (conn->raddr.addr.ss_family == AF_UNIX)
2470		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "\tIs the server running locally and accepting connections on that socket?");
2471	else
2472		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "\tIs the server running on that host and accepting TCP/IP connections?");
2473}
2474
2475/*
2476 * Should we use keepalives?  Returns 1 if yes, 0 if no, and -1 if
2477 * conn->keepalives is set to a value which is not parseable as an
2478 * integer.
2479 */
2480static int
2481useKeepalives(PGconn *conn)
2482{
2483	int			val;
2484
2485	if (conn->keepalives == NULL)
2486		return 1;
2487
2488	if (!pqParseIntParam(conn->keepalives, &val, conn, "keepalives"))
2489		return -1;
2490
2491	return val != 0 ? 1 : 0;
2492}
2493
2494#ifndef WIN32
2495/*
2496 * Set the keepalive idle timer.
2497 */
2498static int
2499setKeepalivesIdle(PGconn *conn)
2500{
2501	int			idle;
2502
2503	if (conn->keepalives_idle == NULL)
2504		return 1;
2505
2506	if (!pqParseIntParam(conn->keepalives_idle, &idle, conn,
2507						 "keepalives_idle"))
2508		return 0;
2509	if (idle < 0)
2510		idle = 0;
2511
2512#ifdef PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE
2513	if (setsockopt(conn->sock, IPPROTO_TCP, PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE,
2514				   (char *) &idle, sizeof(idle)) < 0)
2515	{
2516		char		sebuf[PG_STRERROR_R_BUFLEN];
2517
2518		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s(%s) failed: %s",
2519								"setsockopt",
2520								PG_TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE_STR,
2521								SOCK_STRERROR(SOCK_ERRNO, sebuf, sizeof(sebuf)));
2522		return 0;
2523	}
2524#endif
2525
2526	return 1;
2527}
2528
2529/*
2530 * Set the keepalive interval.
2531 */
2532static int
2533setKeepalivesInterval(PGconn *conn)
2534{
2535	int			interval;
2536
2537	if (conn->keepalives_interval == NULL)
2538		return 1;
2539
2540	if (!pqParseIntParam(conn->keepalives_interval, &interval, conn,
2541						 "keepalives_interval"))
2542		return 0;
2543	if (interval < 0)
2544		interval = 0;
2545
2546#ifdef TCP_KEEPINTVL
2547	if (setsockopt(conn->sock, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_KEEPINTVL,
2548				   (char *) &interval, sizeof(interval)) < 0)
2549	{
2550		char		sebuf[PG_STRERROR_R_BUFLEN];
2551
2552		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s(%s) failed: %s",
2553								"setsockopt",
2554								"TCP_KEEPINTVL",
2555								SOCK_STRERROR(SOCK_ERRNO, sebuf, sizeof(sebuf)));
2556		return 0;
2557	}
2558#endif
2559
2560	return 1;
2561}
2562
2563/*
2564 * Set the count of lost keepalive packets that will trigger a connection
2565 * break.
2566 */
2567static int
2568setKeepalivesCount(PGconn *conn)
2569{
2570	int			count;
2571
2572	if (conn->keepalives_count == NULL)
2573		return 1;
2574
2575	if (!pqParseIntParam(conn->keepalives_count, &count, conn,
2576						 "keepalives_count"))
2577		return 0;
2578	if (count < 0)
2579		count = 0;
2580
2581#ifdef TCP_KEEPCNT
2582	if (setsockopt(conn->sock, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_KEEPCNT,
2583				   (char *) &count, sizeof(count)) < 0)
2584	{
2585		char		sebuf[PG_STRERROR_R_BUFLEN];
2586
2587		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s(%s) failed: %s",
2588								"setsockopt",
2589								"TCP_KEEPCNT",
2590								SOCK_STRERROR(SOCK_ERRNO, sebuf, sizeof(sebuf)));
2591		return 0;
2592	}
2593#endif
2594
2595	return 1;
2596}
2597#else							/* WIN32 */
2598#ifdef SIO_KEEPALIVE_VALS
2599/*
2600 * Enable keepalives and set the keepalive values on Win32,
2601 * where they are always set in one batch.
2602 *
2603 * CAUTION: This needs to be signal safe, since it's used by PQcancel.
2604 */
2605int
2606pqSetKeepalivesWin32(pgsocket sock, int idle, int interval)
2607{
2608	struct tcp_keepalive ka;
2609	DWORD		retsize;
2610
2611	if (idle <= 0)
2612		idle = 2 * 60 * 60;		/* 2 hours = default */
2613	if (interval <= 0)
2614		interval = 1;			/* 1 second = default */
2615
2616	ka.onoff = 1;
2617	ka.keepalivetime = idle * 1000;
2618	ka.keepaliveinterval = interval * 1000;
2619
2620	if (WSAIoctl(sock,
2621				 SIO_KEEPALIVE_VALS,
2622				 (LPVOID) &ka,
2623				 sizeof(ka),
2624				 NULL,
2625				 0,
2626				 &retsize,
2627				 NULL,
2628				 NULL)
2629		!= 0)
2630		return 0;
2631	return 1;
2632}
2633
2634static int
2635prepKeepalivesWin32(PGconn *conn)
2636{
2637	int			idle = -1;
2638	int			interval = -1;
2639
2640	if (conn->keepalives_idle &&
2641		!pqParseIntParam(conn->keepalives_idle, &idle, conn,
2642						 "keepalives_idle"))
2643		return 0;
2644	if (conn->keepalives_interval &&
2645		!pqParseIntParam(conn->keepalives_interval, &interval, conn,
2646						 "keepalives_interval"))
2647		return 0;
2648
2649	if (!pqSetKeepalivesWin32(conn->sock, idle, interval))
2650	{
2651		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s(%s) failed: error code %d",
2652								"WSAIoctl", "SIO_KEEPALIVE_VALS",
2653								WSAGetLastError());
2654		return 0;
2655	}
2656	return 1;
2657}
2658#endif							/* SIO_KEEPALIVE_VALS */
2659#endif							/* WIN32 */
2660
2661/*
2662 * Set the TCP user timeout.
2663 */
2664static int
2665setTCPUserTimeout(PGconn *conn)
2666{
2667	int			timeout;
2668
2669	if (conn->pgtcp_user_timeout == NULL)
2670		return 1;
2671
2672	if (!pqParseIntParam(conn->pgtcp_user_timeout, &timeout, conn,
2673						 "tcp_user_timeout"))
2674		return 0;
2675
2676	if (timeout < 0)
2677		timeout = 0;
2678
2679#ifdef TCP_USER_TIMEOUT
2680	if (setsockopt(conn->sock, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_USER_TIMEOUT,
2681				   (char *) &timeout, sizeof(timeout)) < 0)
2682	{
2683		char		sebuf[256];
2684
2685		libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "%s(%s) failed: %s",
2686								"setsockopt",
2687								"TCP_USER_TIMEOUT",
2688								SOCK_STRERROR(SOCK_ERRNO, sebuf, sizeof(sebuf)));
2689		return 0;
2690	}
2691#endif
2692
2693	return 1;
2694}
2695
2696/* ----------
2697 * pqConnectDBStart -
2698 *		Begin the process of making a connection to the backend.
2699 *
2700 * Returns 1 if successful, 0 if not.
2701 * ----------
2702 */
2703int
2704pqConnectDBStart(PGconn *conn)
2705{
2706	if (!conn)
2707		return 0;
2708
2709	if (!conn->options_valid)
2710		goto connect_errReturn;
2711
2712	/*
2713	 * Check for bad linking to backend-internal versions of src/common
2714	 * functions (see comments in link-canary.c for the reason we need this).
2715	 * Nobody but developers should see this message, so we don't bother
2716	 * translating it.
2717	 */
2718	if (!pg_link_canary_is_frontend())
2719	{
2720		appendPQExpBufferStr(&conn->errorMessage,
2721							 "libpq is incorrectly linked to backend functions\n");
2722		goto connect_errReturn;
2723	}
2724
2725	/* Ensure our buffers are empty */
2726	conn->inStart = conn->inCursor = conn->inEnd = 0;
2727	conn->outCount = 0;
2728
2729	/*
2730	 * Set up to try to connect to the first host.  (Setting whichhost = -1 is
2731	 * a bit of a cheat, but PQconnectPoll will advance it to 0 before
2732	 * anything else looks at it.)
2733	 *
2734	 * Cancel requests are special though, they should only try one host and
2735	 * address, and these fields have already been set up in PQcancelCreate,
2736	 * so leave these fields alone for cancel requests.
2737	 */
2738	if (!conn->cancelRequest)
2739	{
2740		conn->whichhost = -1;
2741		conn->try_next_host = true;
2742		conn->try_next_addr = false;
2743	}
2744
2745	conn->status = CONNECTION_NEEDED;
2746
2747	/* Also reset the target_server_type state if needed */
2748	if (conn->target_server_type == SERVER_TYPE_PREFER_STANDBY_PASS2)
2749		conn->target_server_type = SERVER_TYPE_PREFER_STANDBY;
2750
2751	/*
2752	 * The code for processing CONNECTION_NEEDED state is in PQconnectPoll(),
2753	 * so that it can easily be re-executed if needed again during the
2754	 * asynchronous startup process.  However, we must run it once here,
2755	 * because callers expect a success return from this routine to mean that
2756	 * we are in PGRES_POLLING_WRITING connection state.
2757	 */
2758	if (PQconnectPoll(conn) == PGRES_POLLING_WRITING)
2759		return 1;
2760
2761connect_errReturn:
2762
2763	/*
2764	 * If we managed to open a socket, close it immediately rather than
2765	 * waiting till PQfinish.  (The application cannot have gotten the socket
2766	 * from PQsocket yet, so this doesn't risk breaking anything.)
2767	 */
2768	pqDropConnection(conn, true);
2769	conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;
2770	return 0;
2771}
2772
2773
2774/*
2775 *		pqConnectDBComplete
2776 *
