5245/*
5246 * pqClosePGconn
5247 *	 - properly close a connection to the backend
5248 *
5249 * This should reset or release all transient state, but NOT the connection
5250 * parameters.  On exit, the PGconn should be in condition to start a fresh
5251 * connection with the same parameters (see PQreset()).
5252 */
5253void
5254pqClosePGconn(PGconn *conn)
5255{
5256	/*
5257	 * If possible, send Terminate message to close the connection politely.
5258	 */
5259	sendTerminateConn(conn);
5260
5261	/*
5262	 * Must reset the blocking status so a possible reconnect will work.
5263	 *
5264	 * Don't call PQsetnonblocking() because it will fail if it's unable to
5265	 * flush the connection.
5266	 */
5267	conn->nonblocking = false;
5268
5269	/*
5270	 * Close the connection, reset all transient state, flush I/O buffers.
5271	 * Note that this includes clearing conn's error state; we're no longer
5272	 * interested in any failures associated with the old connection, and we
5273	 * want a clean slate for any new connection attempt.
5274	 */
5275	pqDropConnection(conn, true);
5276	conn->status = CONNECTION_BAD;	/* Well, not really _bad_ - just absent */
5277	conn->asyncStatus = PGASYNC_IDLE;
5278	conn->xactStatus = PQTRANS_IDLE;
5279	conn->pipelineStatus = PQ_PIPELINE_OFF;
5280	pqClearOAuthToken(conn);
5281	pqClearAsyncResult(conn);	/* deallocate result */
5282	pqClearConnErrorState(conn);
5283
5284	/*
5285	 * Release addrinfo, but since cancel requests never change their addrinfo
5286	 * we don't do that. Otherwise we would have to rebuild it during a
5287	 * PQcancelReset.
5288	 */
5289	if (!conn->cancelRequest)
5290		release_conn_addrinfo(conn);
5291
5292	/* Reset all state obtained from server, too */
5293	pqDropServerData(conn);
5294}
5295
5296/*
5297 * PQfinish: properly close a connection to the backend. Also frees
5298 * the PGconn data structure so it shouldn't be re-used after this.
5299 */
5300void
5301PQfinish(PGconn *conn)
5302{
5303	if (conn)
5304	{
5305		pqClosePGconn(conn);
5306		freePGconn(conn);
5307	}
5308}
5309
5310/*
5311 * PQreset: resets the connection to the backend by closing the
5312 * existing connection and creating a new one.
5313 */
5314void
5315PQreset(PGconn *conn)
5316{
5317	if (conn)
5318	{
5319		pqClosePGconn(conn);
5320
5321		if (pqConnectDBStart(conn) && pqConnectDBComplete(conn))
5322		{
5323			/*
5324			 * Notify event procs of successful reset.
5325			 */
5326			int			i;
5327
5328			for (i = 0; i < conn->nEvents; i++)
5329			{
5330				PGEventConnReset evt;
5331
5332				evt.conn = conn;
5333				(void) conn->events[i].proc(PGEVT_CONNRESET, &evt,
5334											conn->events[i].passThrough);
5335			}
5336		}
5337	}
5338}
5339
5340
5341/*
5342 * PQresetStart:
5343 * resets the connection to the backend
5344 * closes the existing connection and makes a new one
5345 * Returns 1 on success, 0 on failure.
5346 */
5347int
5348PQresetStart(PGconn *conn)
5349{
5350	if (conn)
5351	{
5352		pqClosePGconn(conn);
5353
5354		return pqConnectDBStart(conn);
5355	}
5356
5357	return 0;
5358}
5359
5360
5361/*
5362 * PQresetPoll:
5363 * resets the connection to the backend
5364 * closes the existing connection and makes a new one
5365 */
5366PostgresPollingStatusType
5367PQresetPoll(PGconn *conn)
5368{
5369	if (conn)
5370	{
5371		PostgresPollingStatusType status = PQconnectPoll(conn);
5372
5373		if (status == PGRES_POLLING_OK)
5374		{
5375			/*
5376			 * Notify event procs of successful reset.
5377			 */
5378			int			i;
5379
5380			for (i = 0; i < conn->nEvents; i++)
5381			{
5382				PGEventConnReset evt;
5383
5384				evt.conn = conn;
5385				(void) conn->events[i].proc(PGEVT_CONNRESET, &evt,
5386											conn->events[i].passThrough);
5387			}
5388		}
5389
5390		return status;
5391	}
5392
5393	return PGRES_POLLING_FAILED;
5394}
5395
5396/*
5397 * pqPacketSend() -- convenience routine to send a message to server.
5398 *
5399 * pack_type: the single-byte message type code.  (Pass zero for startup
5400 * packets, which have no message type code.)
5401 *
5402 * buf, buf_len: contents of message.  The given length includes only what
5403 * is in buf; the message type and message length fields are added here.
5404 *
5405 * RETURNS: STATUS_ERROR if the write fails, STATUS_OK otherwise.
5406 * SIDE_EFFECTS: may block.
5407 */
5408int
5409pqPacketSend(PGconn *conn, char pack_type,
5410			 const void *buf, size_t buf_len)
5411{
5412	/* Start the message. */
5413	if (pqPutMsgStart(pack_type, conn))
5414		return STATUS_ERROR;
5415
5416	/* Send the message body. */
5417	if (pqPutnchar(buf, buf_len, conn))
5418		return STATUS_ERROR;
5419
5420	/* Finish the message. */
5421	if (pqPutMsgEnd(conn))
5422		return STATUS_ERROR;
5423
5424	/* Flush to ensure backend gets it. */
5425	if (pqFlush(conn))
5426		return STATUS_ERROR;
5427
5428	return STATUS_OK;
5429}
5430
5431#ifdef USE_LDAP
5432
5433#define LDAP_URL	"ldap://"
5434#define LDAP_DEF_PORT	389
5435#define PGLDAP_TIMEOUT 2
5436
5437#define ld_is_sp_tab(x) ((x) == ' ' || (x) == '\t')
5438#define ld_is_nl_cr(x) ((x) == '\r' || (x) == '\n')
5439
5440
5441/*
5442 *		ldapServiceLookup
5443 *
5444 * Search the LDAP URL passed as first argument, treat the result as a
5445 * string of connection options that are parsed and added to the array of
5446 * options passed as second argument.
5447 *
5448 * LDAP URLs must conform to RFC 1959 without escape sequences.
5449 *	ldap://host:port/dn?attributes?scope?filter?extensions
5450 *
5451 * Returns
5452 *	0 if the lookup was successful,
5453 *	1 if the connection to the LDAP server could be established but
5454 *	  the search was unsuccessful,
5455 *	2 if a connection could not be established, and
5456 *	3 if a fatal error occurred.
5457 *
5458 * An error message is appended to *errorMessage for return codes 1 and 3.
5459 */
5460static int
5461ldapServiceLookup(const char *purl, PQconninfoOption *options,
5462				  PQExpBuffer errorMessage)
5463{
5464	int			port = LDAP_DEF_PORT,
5465				scope,
5466				rc,
5467				size,
5468				state,
5469				oldstate,
5470				i;
5471#ifndef WIN32
5472	int			msgid;
5473#endif
5474	bool		found_keyword;
5475	char	   *url,
5476			   *hostname,
5477			   *portstr,
5478			   *endptr,
5479			   *dn,
5480			   *scopestr,
5481			   *filter,
5482			   *result,
5483			   *p,
5484			   *p1 = NULL,
5485			   *optname = NULL,
5486			   *optval = NULL;
5487	char	   *attrs[2] = {NULL, NULL};
5488	LDAP	   *ld = NULL;
5489	LDAPMessage *res,
5490			   *entry;
5491	struct berval **values;
5492	LDAP_TIMEVAL time = {PGLDAP_TIMEOUT, 0};
5493
5494	if ((url = strdup(purl)) == NULL)
5495	{
5496		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
5497		return 3;
5498	}
5499
5500	/*
5501	 * Parse URL components, check for correctness.  Basically, url has '\0'
5502	 * placed at component boundaries and variables are pointed at each
5503	 * component.
5504	 */
5505
5506	if (pg_strncasecmp(url, LDAP_URL, strlen(LDAP_URL)) != 0)
5507	{
5508		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5509						   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": scheme must be ldap://", purl);
5510		free(url);
5511		return 3;
5512	}
5513
5514	/* hostname */
5515	hostname = url + strlen(LDAP_URL);
5516	if (*hostname == '/')		/* no hostname? */
5517		hostname = DefaultHost; /* the default */
5518
5519	/* dn, "distinguished name" */
5520	p = strchr(url + strlen(LDAP_URL), '/');
5521	if (p == NULL || *(p + 1) == '\0' || *(p + 1) == '?')
5522	{
5523		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5524						   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": missing distinguished name",
5525						   purl);
5526		free(url);
5527		return 3;
5528	}
5529	*p = '\0';					/* terminate hostname */
5530	dn = p + 1;
5531
5532	/* attribute */
5533	if ((p = strchr(dn, '?')) == NULL || *(p + 1) == '\0' || *(p + 1) == '?')
5534	{
5535		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5536						   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": must have exactly one attribute",
5537						   purl);
5538		free(url);
5539		return 3;
5540	}
5541	*p = '\0';
5542	attrs[0] = p + 1;
5543
5544	/* scope */
5545	if ((p = strchr(attrs[0], '?')) == NULL || *(p + 1) == '\0' || *(p + 1) == '?')
5546	{
5547		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5548						   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": must have search scope (base/one/sub)",
5549						   purl);
5550		free(url);
5551		return 3;
5552	}
5553	*p = '\0';
5554	scopestr = p + 1;
5555
5556	/* filter */
5557	if ((p = strchr(scopestr, '?')) == NULL || *(p + 1) == '\0' || *(p + 1) == '?')
5558	{
5559		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5560						   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": no filter",
5561						   purl);
5562		free(url);
5563		return 3;
5564	}
5565	*p = '\0';
5566	filter = p + 1;
5567	if ((p = strchr(filter, '?')) != NULL)
5568		*p = '\0';
5569
5570	/* port number? */
5571	if ((p1 = strchr(hostname, ':')) != NULL)
5572	{
5573		long		lport;
5574
5575		*p1 = '\0';
5576		portstr = p1 + 1;
5577		errno = 0;
5578		lport = strtol(portstr, &endptr, 10);
5579		if (*portstr == '\0' || *endptr != '\0' || errno || lport < 0 || lport > 65535)
5580		{
5581			libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5582							   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": invalid port number",
5583							   purl);
5584			free(url);
5585			return 3;
5586		}
5587		port = (int) lport;
5588	}
5589
5590	/* Allow only one attribute */
5591	if (strchr(attrs[0], ',') != NULL)
5592	{
5593		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5594						   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": must have exactly one attribute",
5595						   purl);
5596		free(url);
5597		return 3;
5598	}
5599
5600	/* set scope */
5601	if (pg_strcasecmp(scopestr, "base") == 0)
5602		scope = LDAP_SCOPE_BASE;
5603	else if (pg_strcasecmp(scopestr, "one") == 0)
5604		scope = LDAP_SCOPE_ONELEVEL;
5605	else if (pg_strcasecmp(scopestr, "sub") == 0)
5606		scope = LDAP_SCOPE_SUBTREE;
5607	else
5608	{
5609		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5610						   "invalid LDAP URL \"%s\": must have search scope (base/one/sub)",
5611						   purl);
5612		free(url);
5613		return 3;
5614	}
5615
5616	/* initialize LDAP structure */
5617	if ((ld = ldap_init(hostname, port)) == NULL)
5618	{
5619		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "could not create LDAP structure");
5620		free(url);
5621		return 3;
5622	}
5623
5624	/*
5625	 * Perform an explicit anonymous bind.
5626	 *
5627	 * LDAP does not require that an anonymous bind is performed explicitly,
5628	 * but we want to distinguish between the case where LDAP bind does not
5629	 * succeed within PGLDAP_TIMEOUT seconds (return 2 to continue parsing the
5630	 * service control file) and the case where querying the LDAP server fails
5631	 * (return 1 to end parsing).
5632	 *
5633	 * Unfortunately there is no way of setting a timeout that works for both
5634	 * Windows and OpenLDAP.
5635	 */
5636#ifdef WIN32
5637	/* the nonstandard ldap_connect function performs an anonymous bind */
5638	if (ldap_connect(ld, &time) != LDAP_SUCCESS)
5639	{
5640		/* error or timeout in ldap_connect */
5641		free(url);
5642		ldap_unbind(ld);
5643		return 2;
5644	}
5645#else							/* !WIN32 */
5646	/* in OpenLDAP, use the LDAP_OPT_NETWORK_TIMEOUT option */
5647	if (ldap_set_option(ld, LDAP_OPT_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, &time) != LDAP_SUCCESS)
5648	{
5649		free(url);
5650		ldap_unbind(ld);
5651		return 3;
5652	}
5653
5654	/* anonymous bind */
5655	if ((msgid = ldap_simple_bind(ld, NULL, NULL)) == -1)
5656	{
5657		/* error or network timeout */
5658		free(url);
5659		ldap_unbind(ld);
5660		return 2;
5661	}
5662
5663	/* wait some time for the connection to succeed */
5664	res = NULL;
5665	if ((rc = ldap_result(ld, msgid, LDAP_MSG_ALL, &time, &res)) == -1 ||
5666		res == NULL)
5667	{
5668		/* error or timeout */
5669		if (res != NULL)
5670			ldap_msgfree(res);
5671		free(url);
5672		ldap_unbind(ld);
5673		return 2;
5674	}
5675	ldap_msgfree(res);
5676
5677	/* reset timeout */
5678	time.tv_sec = -1;
5679	if (ldap_set_option(ld, LDAP_OPT_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, &time) != LDAP_SUCCESS)
5680	{
5681		free(url);
5682		ldap_unbind(ld);
5683		return 3;
5684	}
5685#endif							/* WIN32 */
5686
5687	/* search */
5688	res = NULL;
5689	if ((rc = ldap_search_st(ld, dn, scope, filter, attrs, 0, &time, &res))
5690		!= LDAP_SUCCESS)
5691	{
5692		if (res != NULL)
5693			ldap_msgfree(res);
5694		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "lookup on LDAP server failed: %s", ldap_err2string(rc));
5695		ldap_unbind(ld);
5696		free(url);
5697		return 1;
5698	}
5699
5700	/* complain if there was not exactly one result */
5701	if ((rc = ldap_count_entries(ld, res)) != 1)
5702	{
5703		if (rc > 1)
5704			libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "more than one entry found on LDAP lookup");
5705		else
5706			libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "no entry found on LDAP lookup");
5707		ldap_msgfree(res);
5708		ldap_unbind(ld);
5709		free(url);
5710		return 1;
5711	}
5712
5713	/* get entry */
5714	if ((entry = ldap_first_entry(ld, res)) == NULL)
5715	{
5716		/* should never happen */
5717		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "no entry found on LDAP lookup");
5718		ldap_msgfree(res);
5719		ldap_unbind(ld);
5720		free(url);
5721		return 1;
5722	}
5723
5724	/* get values */
5725	if ((values = ldap_get_values_len(ld, entry, attrs[0])) == NULL)
5726	{
5727		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "attribute has no values on LDAP lookup");
5728		ldap_msgfree(res);
5729		ldap_unbind(ld);
5730		free(url);
5731		return 1;
5732	}
5733
5734	ldap_msgfree(res);
5735	free(url);
5736
5737	if (values[0] == NULL)
5738	{
5739		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "attribute has no values on LDAP lookup");
5740		ldap_value_free_len(values);
5741		ldap_unbind(ld);
5742		return 1;
5743	}
5744
5745	/* concatenate values into a single string with newline terminators */
5746	size = 1;					/* for the trailing null */
5747	for (i = 0; values[i] != NULL; i++)
5748	{
5749		if (values[i]->bv_len >= INT_MAX ||
5750			size > (INT_MAX - (values[i]->bv_len + 1)))
5751		{
5752			libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5753							   "connection info string size exceeds the maximum allowed (%d)",
5754							   INT_MAX);
5755			ldap_value_free_len(values);
5756			ldap_unbind(ld);
5757			return 3;
5758		}
5759
5760		size += values[i]->bv_len + 1;
5761	}
5762
5763	if ((result = malloc(size)) == NULL)
5764	{
5765		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
5766		ldap_value_free_len(values);
5767		ldap_unbind(ld);
5768		return 3;
5769	}
5770	p = result;
5771	for (i = 0; values[i] != NULL; i++)
5772	{
5773		memcpy(p, values[i]->bv_val, values[i]->bv_len);
5774		p += values[i]->bv_len;
5775		*(p++) = '\n';
5776	}
5777	*p = '\0';
5778
5779	ldap_value_free_len(values);
5780	ldap_unbind(ld);
5781
5782	/* parse result string */
5783	oldstate = state = 0;
5784	for (p = result; *p != '\0'; ++p)
5785	{
5786		switch (state)
5787		{
5788			case 0:				/* between entries */
5789				if (!ld_is_sp_tab(*p) && !ld_is_nl_cr(*p))
5790				{
5791					optname = p;
5792					state = 1;
5793				}
5794				break;
5795			case 1:				/* in option name */
5796				if (ld_is_sp_tab(*p))
5797				{
5798					*p = '\0';
5799					state = 2;
5800				}
5801				else if (ld_is_nl_cr(*p))
5802				{
5803					libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5804									   "missing \"=\" after \"%s\" in connection info string",
5805									   optname);
5806					free(result);
5807					return 3;
5808				}
5809				else if (*p == '=')
5810				{
5811					*p = '\0';
5812					state = 3;
5813				}
5814				break;
5815			case 2:				/* after option name */
5816				if (*p == '=')
5817				{
5818					state = 3;
5819				}
5820				else if (!ld_is_sp_tab(*p))
5821				{
5822					libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5823									   "missing \"=\" after \"%s\" in connection info string",
5824									   optname);
5825					free(result);
5826					return 3;
5827				}
5828				break;
5829			case 3:				/* before option value */
5830				if (*p == '\'')
5831				{
5832					optval = p + 1;
5833					p1 = p + 1;
5834					state = 5;
5835				}
5836				else if (ld_is_nl_cr(*p))
5837				{
5838					optval = optname + strlen(optname); /* empty */
5839					state = 0;
5840				}
5841				else if (!ld_is_sp_tab(*p))
5842				{
5843					optval = p;
5844					state = 4;
5845				}
5846				break;
5847			case 4:				/* in unquoted option value */
5848				if (ld_is_sp_tab(*p) || ld_is_nl_cr(*p))
5849				{
5850					*p = '\0';
5851					state = 0;
5852				}
5853				break;
5854			case 5:				/* in quoted option value */
5855				if (*p == '\'')
5856				{
5857					*p1 = '\0';
5858					state = 0;
5859				}
5860				else if (*p == '\\')
5861					state = 6;
5862				else
5863					*(p1++) = *p;
5864				break;
5865			case 6:				/* in quoted option value after escape */
5866				*(p1++) = *p;
5867				state = 5;
5868				break;
5869		}
5870
5871		if (state == 0 && oldstate != 0)
5872		{
5873			found_keyword = false;
5874			for (i = 0; options[i].keyword; i++)
5875			{
5876				if (strcmp(options[i].keyword, optname) == 0)
5877				{
5878					if (options[i].val == NULL)
5879					{
5880						options[i].val = strdup(optval);
5881						if (!options[i].val)
5882						{
5883							libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
5884							free(result);
5885							return 3;
5886						}
5887					}
5888					found_keyword = true;
5889					break;
5890				}
5891			}
5892			if (!found_keyword)
5893			{
5894				libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "invalid connection option \"%s\"", optname);
5895				free(result);
5896				return 1;
5897			}
5898			optname = NULL;
5899			optval = NULL;
5900		}
5901		oldstate = state;
5902	}
5903
5904	free(result);
5905
5906	if (state == 5 || state == 6)
5907	{
5908		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
5909						   "unterminated quoted string in connection info string");
5910		return 3;
5911	}
5912
5913	return 0;
5914}
5915
5916#endif							/* USE_LDAP */
5917
5918/*
5919 * parseServiceInfo: if a service name has been given, look it up and absorb
5920 * connection options from it into *options.
5921 *
5922 * Returns 0 on success, nonzero on failure.  On failure, if errorMessage
5923 * isn't null, also store an error message there.  (Note: the only reason
5924 * this function and related ones don't dump core on errorMessage == NULL
5925 * is the undocumented fact that appendPQExpBuffer does nothing when passed
5926 * a null PQExpBuffer pointer.)
5927 */
5928static int
5929parseServiceInfo(PQconninfoOption *options, PQExpBuffer errorMessage)
5930{
5931	const char *service = conninfo_getval(options, "service");
5932	char		serviceFile[MAXPGPATH];
5933	char	   *env;
5934	bool		group_found = false;
5935	int			status;
5936	struct stat stat_buf;
5937
5938	/*
5939	 * We have to special-case the environment variable PGSERVICE here, since
5940	 * this is and should be called before inserting environment defaults for
5941	 * other connection options.
5942	 */
5943	if (service == NULL)
5944		service = getenv("PGSERVICE");
5945
5946	/* If no service name given, nothing to do */
5947	if (service == NULL)
5948		return 0;
5949
5950	/*
5951	 * Try PGSERVICEFILE if specified, else try ~/.pg_service.conf (if that
5952	 * exists).
5953	 */
5954	if ((env = getenv("PGSERVICEFILE")) != NULL)
5955		strlcpy(serviceFile, env, sizeof(serviceFile));
5956	else
5957	{
5958		char		homedir[MAXPGPATH];
5959
5960		if (!pqGetHomeDirectory(homedir, sizeof(homedir)))
5961			goto next_file;
5962		snprintf(serviceFile, MAXPGPATH, "%s/%s", homedir, ".pg_service.conf");
5963		if (stat(serviceFile, &stat_buf) != 0)
5964			goto next_file;
5965	}
5966
5967	status = parseServiceFile(serviceFile, service, options, errorMessage, &group_found);
5968	if (group_found || status != 0)
5969		return status;
5970
5971next_file:
5972
5973	/*
5974	 * This could be used by any application so we can't use the binary
5975	 * location to find our config files.
5976	 */
5977	snprintf(serviceFile, MAXPGPATH, "%s/pg_service.conf",
5978			 getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR") ? getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR") : SYSCONFDIR);
5979	if (stat(serviceFile, &stat_buf) != 0)
5980		goto last_file;
5981
5982	status = parseServiceFile(serviceFile, service, options, errorMessage, &group_found);
5983	if (status != 0)
5984		return status;
5985
5986last_file:
5987	if (!group_found)
5988	{
5989		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "definition of service \"%s\" not found", service);
5990		return 3;
5991	}
5992
5993	return 0;
5994}
5995
5996static int
5997parseServiceFile(const char *serviceFile,
5998				 const char *service,
5999				 PQconninfoOption *options,
6000				 PQExpBuffer errorMessage,
6001				 bool *group_found)
6002{
6003	int			result = 0,
6004				linenr = 0,
6005				i;
6006	FILE	   *f;
6007	char	   *line;
6008	char		buf[1024];
6009
6010	*group_found = false;
6011
6012	f = fopen(serviceFile, "r");
6013	if (f == NULL)
6014	{
6015		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "service file \"%s\" not found", serviceFile);
6016		return 1;
6017	}
6018
6019	while ((line = fgets(buf, sizeof(buf), f)) != NULL)
6020	{
6021		int			len;
6022
6023		linenr++;
6024
6025		if (strlen(line) >= sizeof(buf) - 1)
6026		{
6027			libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6028							   "line %d too long in service file \"%s\"",
6029							   linenr,
6030							   serviceFile);
6031			result = 2;
6032			goto exit;
6033		}
6034
6035		/* ignore whitespace at end of line, especially the newline */
6036		len = strlen(line);
6037		while (len > 0 && isspace((unsigned char) line[len - 1]))
6038			line[--len] = '\0';
6039
6040		/* ignore leading whitespace too */
6041		while (*line && isspace((unsigned char) line[0]))
6042			line++;
6043
6044		/* ignore comments and empty lines */
6045		if (line[0] == '\0' || line[0] == '#')
6046			continue;
6047
6048		/* Check for right groupname */
6049		if (line[0] == '[')
6050		{
6051			if (*group_found)
6052			{
6053				/* end of desired group reached; return success */
6054				goto exit;
6055			}
6056
6057			if (strncmp(line + 1, service, strlen(service)) == 0 &&
6058				line[strlen(service) + 1] == ']')
6059				*group_found = true;
6060			else
6061				*group_found = false;
6062		}
6063		else
6064		{
6065			if (*group_found)
6066			{
6067				/*
6068				 * Finally, we are in the right group and can parse the line
6069				 */
6070				char	   *key,
6071						   *val;
6072				bool		found_keyword;
6073
6074#ifdef USE_LDAP
6075				if (strncmp(line, "ldap", 4) == 0)
6076				{
6077					int			rc = ldapServiceLookup(line, options, errorMessage);
6078
6079					/* if rc = 2, go on reading for fallback */
6080					switch (rc)
6081					{
6082						case 0:
6083							goto exit;
6084						case 1:
6085						case 3:
6086							result = 3;
6087							goto exit;
6088						case 2:
6089							continue;
6090					}
6091				}
6092#endif
6093
6094				key = line;
6095				val = strchr(line, '=');
6096				if (val == NULL)
6097				{
6098					libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6099									   "syntax error in service file \"%s\", line %d",
6100									   serviceFile,
6101									   linenr);
6102					result = 3;
6103					goto exit;
6104				}
6105				*val++ = '\0';
6106
6107				if (strcmp(key, "service") == 0)
6108				{
6109					libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6110									   "nested service specifications not supported in service file \"%s\", line %d",
6111									   serviceFile,
6112									   linenr);
6113					result = 3;
6114					goto exit;
6115				}
6116
6117				/*
6118				 * Set the parameter --- but don't override any previous
6119				 * explicit setting.
6120				 */
6121				found_keyword = false;
6122				for (i = 0; options[i].keyword; i++)
6123				{
6124					if (strcmp(options[i].keyword, key) == 0)
6125					{
6126						if (options[i].val == NULL)
6127							options[i].val = strdup(val);
6128						if (!options[i].val)
6129						{
6130							libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6131							result = 3;
6132							goto exit;
6133						}
6134						found_keyword = true;
6135						break;
6136					}
6137				}
6138
6139				if (!found_keyword)
6140				{
6141					libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6142									   "syntax error in service file \"%s\", line %d",
6143									   serviceFile,
6144									   linenr);
6145					result = 3;
6146					goto exit;
6147				}
6148			}
6149		}
6150	}
6151
6152exit:
6153	fclose(f);
6154
6155	return result;
6156}
6157
6158
6159/*
6160 *		PQconninfoParse
6161 *
6162 * Parse a string like PQconnectdb() would do and return the
6163 * resulting connection options array.  NULL is returned on failure.
6164 * The result contains only options specified directly in the string,
6165 * not any possible default values.
6166 *
6167 * If errmsg isn't NULL, *errmsg is set to NULL on success, or a malloc'd
6168 * string on failure (use PQfreemem to free it).  In out-of-memory conditions
6169 * both *errmsg and the result could be NULL.
6170 *
6171 * NOTE: the returned array is dynamically allocated and should
6172 * be freed when no longer needed via PQconninfoFree().
6173 */
6174PQconninfoOption *
6175PQconninfoParse(const char *conninfo, char **errmsg)
6176{
6177	PQExpBufferData errorBuf;
6178	PQconninfoOption *connOptions;
6179
6180	if (errmsg)
6181		*errmsg = NULL;			/* default */
6182	initPQExpBuffer(&errorBuf);
6183	if (PQExpBufferDataBroken(errorBuf))
6184		return NULL;			/* out of memory already :-( */
6185	connOptions = parse_connection_string(conninfo, &errorBuf, false);
6186	if (connOptions == NULL && errmsg)
6187		*errmsg = errorBuf.data;
6188	else
6189		termPQExpBuffer(&errorBuf);
6190	return connOptions;
6191}
6192
6193/*
6194 * Build a working copy of the constant PQconninfoOptions array.
6195 */
6196static PQconninfoOption *
6197conninfo_init(PQExpBuffer errorMessage)
6198{
6199	PQconninfoOption *options;
6200	PQconninfoOption *opt_dest;
6201	const internalPQconninfoOption *cur_opt;
6202
6203	/*
6204	 * Get enough memory for all options in PQconninfoOptions, even if some
6205	 * end up being filtered out.
6206	 */
6207	options = (PQconninfoOption *) malloc(sizeof(PQconninfoOption) * sizeof(PQconninfoOptions) / sizeof(PQconninfoOptions[0]));
6208	if (options == NULL)
6209	{
6210		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6211		return NULL;
6212	}
6213	opt_dest = options;
6214
6215	for (cur_opt = PQconninfoOptions; cur_opt->keyword; cur_opt++)
6216	{
6217		/* Only copy the public part of the struct, not the full internal */
6218		memcpy(opt_dest, cur_opt, sizeof(PQconninfoOption));
6219		opt_dest++;
6220	}
6221	MemSet(opt_dest, 0, sizeof(PQconninfoOption));
6222
6223	return options;
6224}
6225
6226/*
6227 * Connection string parser
6228 *
6229 * Returns a malloc'd PQconninfoOption array, if parsing is successful.
6230 * Otherwise, NULL is returned and an error message is added to errorMessage.
6231 *
6232 * If use_defaults is true, default values are filled in (from a service file,
6233 * environment variables, etc).
6234 */
6235static PQconninfoOption *
6236parse_connection_string(const char *connstr, PQExpBuffer errorMessage,
6237						bool use_defaults)
6238{
6239	/* Parse as URI if connection string matches URI prefix */
6240	if (uri_prefix_length(connstr) != 0)
6241		return conninfo_uri_parse(connstr, errorMessage, use_defaults);
6242
6243	/* Parse as default otherwise */
6244	return conninfo_parse(connstr, errorMessage, use_defaults);
6245}
6246
6247/*
6248 * Checks if connection string starts with either of the valid URI prefix
6249 * designators.
6250 *
6251 * Returns the URI prefix length, 0 if the string doesn't contain a URI prefix.
6252 *
6253 * XXX this is duplicated in psql/common.c.
6254 */
6255static int
6256uri_prefix_length(const char *connstr)
6257{
6258	if (strncmp(connstr, uri_designator,
6259				sizeof(uri_designator) - 1) == 0)
6260		return sizeof(uri_designator) - 1;
6261
6262	if (strncmp(connstr, short_uri_designator,
6263				sizeof(short_uri_designator) - 1) == 0)
6264		return sizeof(short_uri_designator) - 1;
6265
6266	return 0;
6267}
6268
6269/*
6270 * Recognized connection string either starts with a valid URI prefix or
6271 * contains a "=" in it.
6272 *
6273 * Must be consistent with parse_connection_string: anything for which this
6274 * returns true should at least look like it's parseable by that routine.
6275 *
6276 * XXX this is duplicated in psql/common.c
6277 */
6278static bool
6279recognized_connection_string(const char *connstr)
6280{
6281	return uri_prefix_length(connstr) != 0 || strchr(connstr, '=') != NULL;
6282}
6283
6284/*
6285 * Subroutine for parse_connection_string
6286 *
6287 * Deal with a string containing key=value pairs.
6288 */
6289static PQconninfoOption *
6290conninfo_parse(const char *conninfo, PQExpBuffer errorMessage,
6291			   bool use_defaults)
6292{
6293	char	   *pname;
6294	char	   *pval;
6295	char	   *buf;
6296	char	   *cp;
6297	char	   *cp2;
6298	PQconninfoOption *options;
6299
6300	/* Make a working copy of PQconninfoOptions */
6301	options = conninfo_init(errorMessage);
6302	if (options == NULL)
6303		return NULL;
6304
6305	/* Need a modifiable copy of the input string */
6306	if ((buf = strdup(conninfo)) == NULL)
6307	{
6308		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6309		PQconninfoFree(options);
6310		return NULL;
6311	}
6312	cp = buf;
6313
6314	while (*cp)
6315	{
6316		/* Skip blanks before the parameter name */
6317		if (isspace((unsigned char) *cp))
6318		{
6319			cp++;
6320			continue;
6321		}
6322
6323		/* Get the parameter name */
6324		pname = cp;
6325		while (*cp)
6326		{
6327			if (*cp == '=')
6328				break;
6329			if (isspace((unsigned char) *cp))
6330			{
6331				*cp++ = '\0';
6332				while (*cp)
6333				{
6334					if (!isspace((unsigned char) *cp))
6335						break;
6336					cp++;
6337				}
6338				break;
6339			}
6340			cp++;
6341		}
6342
6343		/* Check that there is a following '=' */
6344		if (*cp != '=')
6345		{
6346			libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6347							   "missing \"=\" after \"%s\" in connection info string",
6348							   pname);
6349			PQconninfoFree(options);
6350			free(buf);
6351			return NULL;
6352		}
6353		*cp++ = '\0';
6354
6355		/* Skip blanks after the '=' */
6356		while (*cp)
6357		{
6358			if (!isspace((unsigned char) *cp))
6359				break;
6360			cp++;
6361		}
6362
6363		/* Get the parameter value */
6364		pval = cp;
6365
6366		if (*cp != '\'')
6367		{
6368			cp2 = pval;
6369			while (*cp)
6370			{
6371				if (isspace((unsigned char) *cp))
6372				{
6373					*cp++ = '\0';
6374					break;
6375				}
6376				if (*cp == '\\')
6377				{
6378					cp++;
6379					if (*cp != '\0')
6380						*cp2++ = *cp++;
6381				}
6382				else
6383					*cp2++ = *cp++;
6384			}
6385			*cp2 = '\0';
6386		}
6387		else
6388		{
6389			cp2 = pval;
6390			cp++;
6391			for (;;)
6392			{
6393				if (*cp == '\0')
6394				{
6395					libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "unterminated quoted string in connection info string");
6396					PQconninfoFree(options);
6397					free(buf);
6398					return NULL;
6399				}
6400				if (*cp == '\\')
6401				{
6402					cp++;
6403					if (*cp != '\0')
6404						*cp2++ = *cp++;
6405					continue;
6406				}
6407				if (*cp == '\'')
6408				{
6409					*cp2 = '\0';
6410					cp++;
6411					break;
6412				}
6413				*cp2++ = *cp++;
6414			}
6415		}
6416
6417		/*
6418		 * Now that we have the name and the value, store the record.
6419		 */
6420		if (!conninfo_storeval(options, pname, pval, errorMessage, false, false))
6421		{
6422			PQconninfoFree(options);
6423			free(buf);
6424			return NULL;
6425		}
6426	}
6427
6428	/* Done with the modifiable input string */
6429	free(buf);
6430
6431	/*
6432	 * Add in defaults if the caller wants that.
6433	 */
6434	if (use_defaults)
6435	{
6436		if (!conninfo_add_defaults(options, errorMessage))
6437		{
6438			PQconninfoFree(options);
6439			return NULL;
6440		}
6441	}
6442
6443	return options;
6444}
6445
6446/*
6447 * Conninfo array parser routine
6448 *
6449 * If successful, a malloc'd PQconninfoOption array is returned.
6450 * If not successful, NULL is returned and an error message is
6451 * appended to errorMessage.
6452 * Defaults are supplied (from a service file, environment variables, etc)
6453 * for unspecified options, but only if use_defaults is true.
6454 *
6455 * If expand_dbname is non-zero, and the value passed for the first occurrence
6456 * of "dbname" keyword is a connection string (as indicated by
6457 * recognized_connection_string) then parse and process it, overriding any
6458 * previously processed conflicting keywords. Subsequent keywords will take
6459 * precedence, however. In-tree programs generally specify expand_dbname=true,
6460 * so command-line arguments naming a database can use a connection string.
6461 * Some code acquires arbitrary database names from known-literal sources like
6462 * PQdb(), PQconninfoParse() and pg_database.datname.  When connecting to such
6463 * a database, in-tree code first wraps the name in a connection string.
6464 */
6465static PQconninfoOption *
6466conninfo_array_parse(const char *const *keywords, const char *const *values,
6467					 PQExpBuffer errorMessage, bool use_defaults,
6468					 int expand_dbname)
6469{
6470	PQconninfoOption *options;
6471	PQconninfoOption *dbname_options = NULL;
6472	PQconninfoOption *option;
6473	int			i = 0;
6474
6475	/*
6476	 * If expand_dbname is non-zero, check keyword "dbname" to see if val is
6477	 * actually a recognized connection string.
6478	 */
6479	while (expand_dbname && keywords[i])
6480	{
6481		const char *pname = keywords[i];
6482		const char *pvalue = values[i];
6483
6484		/* first find "dbname" if any */
6485		if (strcmp(pname, "dbname") == 0 && pvalue)
6486		{
6487			/*
6488			 * If value is a connection string, parse it, but do not use
6489			 * defaults here -- those get picked up later. We only want to
6490			 * override for those parameters actually passed.
6491			 */
6492			if (recognized_connection_string(pvalue))
6493			{
6494				dbname_options = parse_connection_string(pvalue, errorMessage, false);
6495				if (dbname_options == NULL)
6496					return NULL;
6497			}
6498			break;
6499		}
6500		++i;
6501	}
6502
6503	/* Make a working copy of PQconninfoOptions */
6504	options = conninfo_init(errorMessage);
6505	if (options == NULL)
6506	{
6507		PQconninfoFree(dbname_options);
6508		return NULL;
6509	}
6510
6511	/* Parse the keywords/values arrays */
6512	i = 0;
6513	while (keywords[i])
6514	{
6515		const char *pname = keywords[i];
6516		const char *pvalue = values[i];
6517
6518		if (pvalue != NULL && pvalue[0] != '\0')
6519		{
6520			/* Search for the param record */
6521			for (option = options; option->keyword != NULL; option++)
6522			{
6523				if (strcmp(option->keyword, pname) == 0)
6524					break;
6525			}
6526
6527			/* Check for invalid connection option */
6528			if (option->keyword == NULL)
6529			{
6530				libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "invalid connection option \"%s\"", pname);
6531				PQconninfoFree(options);
6532				PQconninfoFree(dbname_options);
6533				return NULL;
6534			}
6535
6536			/*
6537			 * If we are on the first dbname parameter, and we have a parsed
6538			 * connection string, copy those parameters across, overriding any
6539			 * existing previous settings.
6540			 */
6541			if (strcmp(pname, "dbname") == 0 && dbname_options)
6542			{
6543				PQconninfoOption *str_option;
6544
6545				for (str_option = dbname_options; str_option->keyword != NULL; str_option++)
6546				{
6547					if (str_option->val != NULL)
6548					{
6549						int			k;
6550
6551						for (k = 0; options[k].keyword; k++)
6552						{
6553							if (strcmp(options[k].keyword, str_option->keyword) == 0)
6554							{
6555								free(options[k].val);
6556								options[k].val = strdup(str_option->val);
6557								if (!options[k].val)
6558								{
6559									libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6560									PQconninfoFree(options);
6561									PQconninfoFree(dbname_options);
6562									return NULL;
6563								}
6564								break;
6565							}
6566						}
6567					}
6568				}
6569
6570				/*
6571				 * Forget the parsed connection string, so that any subsequent
6572				 * dbname parameters will not be expanded.
6573				 */
6574				PQconninfoFree(dbname_options);
6575				dbname_options = NULL;
6576			}
6577			else
6578			{
6579				/*
6580				 * Store the value, overriding previous settings
6581				 */
6582				free(option->val);
6583				option->val = strdup(pvalue);
6584				if (!option->val)
6585				{
6586					libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6587					PQconninfoFree(options);
6588					PQconninfoFree(dbname_options);
6589					return NULL;
6590				}
6591			}
6592		}
6593		++i;
6594	}
6595	PQconninfoFree(dbname_options);
6596
6597	/*
6598	 * Add in defaults if the caller wants that.
6599	 */
6600	if (use_defaults)
6601	{
6602		if (!conninfo_add_defaults(options, errorMessage))
6603		{
6604			PQconninfoFree(options);
6605			return NULL;
6606		}
6607	}
6608
6609	return options;
6610}
6611
6612/*
6613 * Add the default values for any unspecified options to the connection
6614 * options array.
6615 *
6616 * Defaults are obtained from a service file, environment variables, etc.
6617 *
6618 * Returns true if successful, otherwise false; errorMessage, if supplied,
6619 * is filled in upon failure.  Note that failure to locate a default value
6620 * is not an error condition here --- we just leave the option's value as
6621 * NULL.
6622 */
6623static bool
6624conninfo_add_defaults(PQconninfoOption *options, PQExpBuffer errorMessage)
6625{
6626	PQconninfoOption *option;
6627	PQconninfoOption *sslmode_default = NULL,
6628			   *sslrootcert = NULL;
6629	char	   *tmp;
6630
6631	/*
6632	 * If there's a service spec, use it to obtain any not-explicitly-given
6633	 * parameters.  Ignore error if no error message buffer is passed because
6634	 * there is no way to pass back the failure message.
6635	 */
6636	if (parseServiceInfo(options, errorMessage) != 0 && errorMessage)
6637		return false;
6638
6639	/*
6640	 * Get the fallback resources for parameters not specified in the conninfo
6641	 * string nor the service.
6642	 */
6643	for (option = options; option->keyword != NULL; option++)
6644	{
6645		if (strcmp(option->keyword, "sslrootcert") == 0)
6646			sslrootcert = option;	/* save for later */
6647
6648		if (option->val != NULL)
6649			continue;			/* Value was in conninfo or service */
6650
6651		/*
6652		 * Try to get the environment variable fallback
6653		 */
6654		if (option->envvar != NULL)
6655		{
6656			if ((tmp = getenv(option->envvar)) != NULL)
6657			{
6658				option->val = strdup(tmp);
6659				if (!option->val)
6660				{
6661					if (errorMessage)
6662						libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6663					return false;
6664				}
6665				continue;
6666			}
6667		}
6668
6669		/*
6670		 * Interpret the deprecated PGREQUIRESSL environment variable.  Per
6671		 * tradition, translate values starting with "1" to sslmode=require,
6672		 * and ignore other values.  Given both PGREQUIRESSL=1 and PGSSLMODE,
6673		 * PGSSLMODE takes precedence; the opposite was true before v9.3.
6674		 */
6675		if (strcmp(option->keyword, "sslmode") == 0)
6676		{
6677			const char *requiresslenv = getenv("PGREQUIRESSL");
6678
6679			if (requiresslenv != NULL && requiresslenv[0] == '1')
6680			{
6681				option->val = strdup("require");
6682				if (!option->val)
6683				{
6684					if (errorMessage)
6685						libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6686					return false;
6687				}
6688				continue;
6689			}
6690
6691			/*
6692			 * sslmode is not specified. Let it be filled in with the compiled
6693			 * default for now, but if sslrootcert=system, we'll override the
6694			 * default later before returning.
6695			 */
6696			sslmode_default = option;
6697		}
6698
6699		/*
6700		 * No environment variable specified or the variable isn't set - try
6701		 * compiled-in default
6702		 */
6703		if (option->compiled != NULL)
6704		{
6705			option->val = strdup(option->compiled);
6706			if (!option->val)
6707			{
6708				if (errorMessage)
6709					libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6710				return false;
6711			}
6712			continue;
6713		}
6714
6715		/*
6716		 * Special handling for "user" option.  Note that if pg_fe_getauthname
6717		 * fails, we just leave the value as NULL; there's no need for this to
6718		 * be an error condition if the caller provides a user name.  The only
6719		 * reason we do this now at all is so that callers of PQconndefaults
6720		 * will see a correct default (barring error, of course).
6721		 */
6722		if (strcmp(option->keyword, "user") == 0)
6723		{
6724			option->val = pg_fe_getauthname(NULL);
6725			continue;
6726		}
6727	}
6728
6729	/*
6730	 * Special handling for sslrootcert=system with no sslmode explicitly
6731	 * defined. In this case we want to strengthen the default sslmode to
6732	 * verify-full.
6733	 */
6734	if (sslmode_default && sslrootcert)
6735	{
6736		if (sslrootcert->val && strcmp(sslrootcert->val, "system") == 0)
6737		{
6738			free(sslmode_default->val);
6739
6740			sslmode_default->val = strdup("verify-full");
6741			if (!sslmode_default->val)
6742			{
6743				if (errorMessage)
6744					libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6745				return false;
6746			}
6747		}
6748	}
6749
6750	return true;
6751}
6752
6753/*
6754 * Subroutine for parse_connection_string
6755 *
6756 * Deal with a URI connection string.
6757 */
6758static PQconninfoOption *
6759conninfo_uri_parse(const char *uri, PQExpBuffer errorMessage,
6760				   bool use_defaults)
6761{
6762	PQconninfoOption *options;
6763
6764	/* Make a working copy of PQconninfoOptions */
6765	options = conninfo_init(errorMessage);
6766	if (options == NULL)
6767		return NULL;
6768
6769	if (!conninfo_uri_parse_options(options, uri, errorMessage))
6770	{
6771		PQconninfoFree(options);
6772		return NULL;
6773	}
6774
6775	/*
6776	 * Add in defaults if the caller wants that.
6777	 */
6778	if (use_defaults)
6779	{
6780		if (!conninfo_add_defaults(options, errorMessage))
6781		{
6782			PQconninfoFree(options);
6783			return NULL;
6784		}
6785	}
6786
6787	return options;
6788}
6789
6790/*
6791 * conninfo_uri_parse_options
6792 *		Actual URI parser.
6793 *
6794 * If successful, returns true while the options array is filled with parsed
6795 * options from the URI.
6796 * If not successful, returns false and fills errorMessage accordingly.
6797 *
6798 * Parses the connection URI string in 'uri' according to the URI syntax (RFC
6799 * 3986):
6800 *
6801 * postgresql://[user[:password]@][netloc][:port][/dbname][?param1=value1&...]
6802 *
6803 * where "netloc" is a hostname, an IPv4 address, or an IPv6 address surrounded
6804 * by literal square brackets.  As an extension, we also allow multiple
6805 * netloc[:port] specifications, separated by commas:
6806 *
6807 * postgresql://[user[:password]@][netloc][:port][,...][/dbname][?param1=value1&...]
6808 *
6809 * Any of the URI parts might use percent-encoding (%xy).
6810 */
6811static bool
6812conninfo_uri_parse_options(PQconninfoOption *options, const char *uri,
6813						   PQExpBuffer errorMessage)
6814{
6815	int			prefix_len;
6816	char	   *p;
6817	char	   *buf = NULL;
6818	char	   *start;
6819	char		prevchar = '\0';
6820	char	   *user = NULL;
6821	char	   *host = NULL;
6822	bool		retval = false;
6823	PQExpBufferData hostbuf;
6824	PQExpBufferData portbuf;
6825
6826	initPQExpBuffer(&hostbuf);
6827	initPQExpBuffer(&portbuf);
6828	if (PQExpBufferDataBroken(hostbuf) || PQExpBufferDataBroken(portbuf))
6829	{
6830		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6831		goto cleanup;
6832	}
6833
6834	/* need a modifiable copy of the input URI */
6835	buf = strdup(uri);
6836	if (buf == NULL)
6837	{
6838		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
6839		goto cleanup;
6840	}
6841	start = buf;
6842
6843	/* Skip the URI prefix */
6844	prefix_len = uri_prefix_length(uri);
6845	if (prefix_len == 0)
6846	{
6847		/* Should never happen */
6848		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6849						   "invalid URI propagated to internal parser routine: \"%s\"",
6850						   uri);
6851		goto cleanup;
6852	}
6853	start += prefix_len;
6854	p = start;
6855
6856	/* Look ahead for possible user credentials designator */
6857	while (*p && *p != '@' && *p != '/')
6858		++p;
6859	if (*p == '@')
6860	{
6861		/*
6862		 * Found username/password designator, so URI should be of the form
6863		 * "scheme://user[:password]@[netloc]".
6864		 */
6865		user = start;
6866
6867		p = user;
6868		while (*p != ':' && *p != '@')
6869			++p;
6870
6871		/* Save last char and cut off at end of user name */
6872		prevchar = *p;
6873		*p = '\0';
6874
6875		if (*user &&
6876			!conninfo_storeval(options, "user", user,
6877							   errorMessage, false, true))
6878			goto cleanup;
6879
6880		if (prevchar == ':')
6881		{
6882			const char *password = p + 1;
6883
6884			while (*p != '@')
6885				++p;
6886			*p = '\0';
6887
6888			if (*password &&
6889				!conninfo_storeval(options, "password", password,
6890								   errorMessage, false, true))
6891				goto cleanup;
6892		}
6893
6894		/* Advance past end of parsed user name or password token */
6895		++p;
6896	}
6897	else
6898	{
6899		/*
6900		 * No username/password designator found.  Reset to start of URI.
6901		 */
6902		p = start;
6903	}
6904
6905	/*
6906	 * There may be multiple netloc[:port] pairs, each separated from the next
6907	 * by a comma.  When we initially enter this loop, "p" has been
6908	 * incremented past optional URI credential information at this point and
6909	 * now points at the "netloc" part of the URI.  On subsequent loop
6910	 * iterations, "p" has been incremented past the comma separator and now
6911	 * points at the start of the next "netloc".
6912	 */
6913	for (;;)
6914	{
6915		/*
6916		 * Look for IPv6 address.
6917		 */
6918		if (*p == '[')
6919		{
6920			host = ++p;
6921			while (*p && *p != ']')
6922				++p;
6923			if (!*p)
6924			{
6925				libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6926								   "end of string reached when looking for matching \"]\" in IPv6 host address in URI: \"%s\"",
6927								   uri);
6928				goto cleanup;
6929			}
6930			if (p == host)
6931			{
6932				libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6933								   "IPv6 host address may not be empty in URI: \"%s\"",
6934								   uri);
6935				goto cleanup;
6936			}
6937
6938			/* Cut off the bracket and advance */
6939			*(p++) = '\0';
6940
6941			/*
6942			 * The address may be followed by a port specifier or a slash or a
6943			 * query or a separator comma.
6944			 */
6945			if (*p && *p != ':' && *p != '/' && *p != '?' && *p != ',')
6946			{
6947				libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
6948								   "unexpected character \"%c\" at position %d in URI (expected \":\" or \"/\"): \"%s\"",
6949								   *p, (int) (p - buf + 1), uri);
6950				goto cleanup;
6951			}
6952		}
6953		else
6954		{
6955			/* not an IPv6 address: DNS-named or IPv4 netloc */
6956			host = p;
6957
6958			/*
6959			 * Look for port specifier (colon) or end of host specifier
6960			 * (slash) or query (question mark) or host separator (comma).
6961			 */
6962			while (*p && *p != ':' && *p != '/' && *p != '?' && *p != ',')
6963				++p;
6964		}
6965
6966		/* Save the hostname terminator before we null it */
6967		prevchar = *p;
6968		*p = '\0';
6969
6970		appendPQExpBufferStr(&hostbuf, host);
6971
6972		if (prevchar == ':')
6973		{
6974			const char *port = ++p; /* advance past host terminator */
6975
6976			while (*p && *p != '/' && *p != '?' && *p != ',')
6977				++p;
6978
6979			prevchar = *p;
6980			*p = '\0';
6981
6982			appendPQExpBufferStr(&portbuf, port);
6983		}
6984
6985		if (prevchar != ',')
6986			break;
6987		++p;					/* advance past comma separator */
6988		appendPQExpBufferChar(&hostbuf, ',');
6989		appendPQExpBufferChar(&portbuf, ',');
6990	}
6991
6992	/* Save final values for host and port. */
6993	if (PQExpBufferDataBroken(hostbuf) || PQExpBufferDataBroken(portbuf))
6994		goto cleanup;
6995	if (hostbuf.data[0] &&
6996		!conninfo_storeval(options, "host", hostbuf.data,
6997						   errorMessage, false, true))
6998		goto cleanup;
6999	if (portbuf.data[0] &&
7000		!conninfo_storeval(options, "port", portbuf.data,
7001						   errorMessage, false, true))
7002		goto cleanup;
7003
7004	if (prevchar && prevchar != '?')
7005	{
7006		const char *dbname = ++p;	/* advance past host terminator */
7007
7008		/* Look for query parameters */
7009		while (*p && *p != '?')
7010			++p;
7011
7012		prevchar = *p;
7013		*p = '\0';
7014
7015		/*
7016		 * Avoid setting dbname to an empty string, as it forces the default
7017		 * value (username) and ignores $PGDATABASE, as opposed to not setting
7018		 * it at all.
7019		 */
7020		if (*dbname &&
7021			!conninfo_storeval(options, "dbname", dbname,
7022							   errorMessage, false, true))
7023			goto cleanup;
7024	}
7025
7026	if (prevchar)
7027	{
7028		++p;					/* advance past terminator */
7029
7030		if (!conninfo_uri_parse_params(p, options, errorMessage))
7031			goto cleanup;
7032	}
7033
7034	/* everything parsed okay */
7035	retval = true;
7036
7037cleanup:
7038	termPQExpBuffer(&hostbuf);
7039	termPQExpBuffer(&portbuf);
7040	free(buf);
7041	return retval;
7042}
7043
7044/*
7045 * Connection URI parameters parser routine
7046 *
7047 * If successful, returns true while connOptions is filled with parsed
7048 * parameters.  Otherwise, returns false and fills errorMessage appropriately.
7049 *
7050 * Destructively modifies 'params' buffer.
7051 */
7052static bool
7053conninfo_uri_parse_params(char *params,
7054						  PQconninfoOption *connOptions,
7055						  PQExpBuffer errorMessage)
7056{
7057	while (*params)
7058	{
7059		char	   *keyword = params;
7060		char	   *value = NULL;
7061		char	   *p = params;
7062		bool		malloced = false;
7063		int			oldmsglen;
7064
7065		/*
7066		 * Scan the params string for '=' and '&', marking the end of keyword
7067		 * and value respectively.
7068		 */
7069		for (;;)
7070		{
7071			if (*p == '=')
7072			{
7073				/* Was there '=' already? */
7074				if (value != NULL)
7075				{
7076					libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
7077									   "extra key/value separator \"=\" in URI query parameter: \"%s\"",
7078									   keyword);
7079					return false;
7080				}
7081				/* Cut off keyword, advance to value */
7082				*p++ = '\0';
7083				value = p;
7084			}
7085			else if (*p == '&' || *p == '\0')
7086			{
7087				/*
7088				 * If not at the end, cut off value and advance; leave p
7089				 * pointing to start of the next parameter, if any.
7090				 */
7091				if (*p != '\0')
7092					*p++ = '\0';
7093				/* Was there '=' at all? */
7094				if (value == NULL)
7095				{
7096					libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
7097									   "missing key/value separator \"=\" in URI query parameter: \"%s\"",
7098									   keyword);
7099					return false;
7100				}
7101				/* Got keyword and value, go process them. */
7102				break;
7103			}
7104			else
7105				++p;			/* Advance over all other bytes. */
7106		}
7107
7108		keyword = conninfo_uri_decode(keyword, errorMessage);
7109		if (keyword == NULL)
7110		{
7111			/* conninfo_uri_decode already set an error message */
7112			return false;
7113		}
7114		value = conninfo_uri_decode(value, errorMessage);
7115		if (value == NULL)
7116		{
7117			/* conninfo_uri_decode already set an error message */
7118			free(keyword);
7119			return false;
7120		}
7121		malloced = true;
7122
7123		/*
7124		 * Special keyword handling for improved JDBC compatibility.
7125		 */
7126		if (strcmp(keyword, "ssl") == 0 &&
7127			strcmp(value, "true") == 0)
7128		{
7129			free(keyword);
7130			free(value);
7131			malloced = false;
7132
7133			keyword = "sslmode";
7134			value = "require";
7135		}
7136
7137		/*
7138		 * Store the value if the corresponding option exists; ignore
7139		 * otherwise.  At this point both keyword and value are not
7140		 * URI-encoded.
7141		 */
7142		oldmsglen = errorMessage->len;
7143		if (!conninfo_storeval(connOptions, keyword, value,
7144							   errorMessage, true, false))
7145		{
7146			/* Insert generic message if conninfo_storeval didn't give one. */
7147			if (errorMessage->len == oldmsglen)
7148				libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
7149								   "invalid URI query parameter: \"%s\"",
7150								   keyword);
7151			/* And fail. */
7152			if (malloced)
7153			{
7154				free(keyword);
7155				free(value);
7156			}
7157			return false;
7158		}
7159
7160		if (malloced)
7161		{
7162			free(keyword);
7163			free(value);
7164		}
7165
7166		/* Proceed to next key=value pair, if any */
7167		params = p;
7168	}
7169
7170	return true;
7171}
7172
7173/*
7174 * Connection URI decoder routine
7175 *
7176 * If successful, returns the malloc'd decoded string.
7177 * If not successful, returns NULL and fills errorMessage accordingly.
7178 *
7179 * The string is decoded by replacing any percent-encoded tokens with
7180 * corresponding characters, while preserving any non-encoded characters.  A
7181 * percent-encoded token is a character triplet: a percent sign, followed by a
7182 * pair of hexadecimal digits (0-9A-F), where lower- and upper-case letters are
7183 * treated identically.
7184 */
7185static char *
7186conninfo_uri_decode(const char *str, PQExpBuffer errorMessage)
7187{
7188	char	   *buf;			/* result */
7189	char	   *p;				/* output location */
7190	const char *q = str;		/* input location */
7191
7192	buf = malloc(strlen(str) + 1);
7193	if (buf == NULL)
7194	{
7195		libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
7196		return NULL;
7197	}
7198	p = buf;
7199
7200	/* skip leading whitespaces */
7201	for (const char *s = q; *s == ' '; s++)
7202	{
7203		q++;
7204		continue;
7205	}
7206
7207	for (;;)
7208	{
7209		if (*q != '%')
7210		{
7211			/* if found a whitespace or NUL, the string ends */
7212			if (*q == ' ' || *q == '\0')
7213				goto end;
7214
7215			/* copy character */
7216			*(p++) = *(q++);
7217		}
7218		else
7219		{
7220			int			hi;
7221			int			lo;
7222			int			c;
7223
7224			++q;				/* skip the percent sign itself */
7225
7226			/*
7227			 * Possible EOL will be caught by the first call to
7228			 * get_hexdigit(), so we never dereference an invalid q pointer.
7229			 */
7230			if (!(get_hexdigit(*q++, &hi) && get_hexdigit(*q++, &lo)))
7231			{
7232				libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
7233								   "invalid percent-encoded token: \"%s\"",
7234								   str);
7235				free(buf);
7236				return NULL;
7237			}
7238
7239			c = (hi << 4) | lo;
7240			if (c == 0)
7241			{
7242				libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
7243								   "forbidden value %%00 in percent-encoded value: \"%s\"",
7244								   str);
7245				free(buf);
7246				return NULL;
7247			}
7248			*(p++) = c;
7249		}
7250	}
7251
7252end:
7253
7254	/* skip trailing whitespaces */
7255	for (const char *s = q; *s == ' '; s++)
7256	{
7257		q++;
7258		continue;
7259	}
7260
7261	/* Not at the end of the string yet?  Fail. */
7262	if (*q != '\0')
7263	{
7264		libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
7265						   "unexpected spaces found in \"%s\", use percent-encoded spaces (%%20) instead",
7266						   str);
7267		free(buf);
7268		return NULL;
7269	}
7270
7271	/* Copy NUL terminator */
7272	*p = '\0';
7273
7274	return buf;
7275}
7276
7277/*
7278 * Convert hexadecimal digit character to its integer value.
7279 *
7280 * If successful, returns true and value is filled with digit's base 16 value.
7281 * If not successful, returns false.
7282 *
7283 * Lower- and upper-case letters in the range A-F are treated identically.
7284 */
7285static bool
7286get_hexdigit(char digit, int *value)
7287{
7288	if ('0' <= digit && digit <= '9')
7289		*value = digit - '0';
7290	else if ('A' <= digit && digit <= 'F')
7291		*value = digit - 'A' + 10;
7292	else if ('a' <= digit && digit <= 'f')
7293		*value = digit - 'a' + 10;
7294	else
7295		return false;
7296
7297	return true;
7298}
7299
7300/*
7301 * Find an option value corresponding to the keyword in the connOptions array.
7302 *
7303 * If successful, returns a pointer to the corresponding option's value.
7304 * If not successful, returns NULL.
7305 */
7306static const char *
7307conninfo_getval(PQconninfoOption *connOptions,
7308				const char *keyword)
7309{
7310	PQconninfoOption *option;
7311
7312	option = conninfo_find(connOptions, keyword);
7313
7314	return option ? option->val : NULL;
7315}
7316
7317/*
7318 * Store a (new) value for an option corresponding to the keyword in
7319 * connOptions array.
7320 *
7321 * If uri_decode is true, the value is URI-decoded.  The keyword is always
7322 * assumed to be non URI-encoded.
7323 *
7324 * If successful, returns a pointer to the corresponding PQconninfoOption,
7325 * which value is replaced with a strdup'd copy of the passed value string.
7326 * The existing value for the option is free'd before replacing, if any.
7327 *
7328 * If not successful, returns NULL and fills errorMessage accordingly.
7329 * However, if the reason of failure is an invalid keyword being passed and
7330 * ignoreMissing is true, errorMessage will be left untouched.
7331 */
7332static PQconninfoOption *
7333conninfo_storeval(PQconninfoOption *connOptions,
7334				  const char *keyword, const char *value,
7335				  PQExpBuffer errorMessage, bool ignoreMissing,
7336				  bool uri_decode)
7337{
7338	PQconninfoOption *option;
7339	char	   *value_copy;
7340
7341	/*
7342	 * For backwards compatibility, requiressl=1 gets translated to
7343	 * sslmode=require, and requiressl=0 gets translated to sslmode=prefer
7344	 * (which is the default for sslmode).
7345	 */
7346	if (strcmp(keyword, "requiressl") == 0)
7347	{
7348		keyword = "sslmode";
7349		if (value[0] == '1')
7350			value = "require";
7351		else
7352			value = "prefer";
7353	}
7354
7355	option = conninfo_find(connOptions, keyword);
7356	if (option == NULL)
7357	{
7358		if (!ignoreMissing)
7359			libpq_append_error(errorMessage,
7360							   "invalid connection option \"%s\"",
7361							   keyword);
7362		return NULL;
7363	}
7364
7365	if (uri_decode)
7366	{
7367		value_copy = conninfo_uri_decode(value, errorMessage);
7368		if (value_copy == NULL)
7369			/* conninfo_uri_decode already set an error message */
7370			return NULL;
7371	}
7372	else
7373	{
7374		value_copy = strdup(value);
7375		if (value_copy == NULL)
7376		{
7377			libpq_append_error(errorMessage, "out of memory");
7378			return NULL;
7379		}
7380	}
7381
7382	free(option->val);
7383	option->val = value_copy;
7384
7385	return option;
7386}
7387
7388/*
7389 * Find a PQconninfoOption option corresponding to the keyword in the
7390 * connOptions array.
7391 *
7392 * If successful, returns a pointer to the corresponding PQconninfoOption
7393 * structure.
7394 * If not successful, returns NULL.
7395 */
7396static PQconninfoOption *
7397conninfo_find(PQconninfoOption *connOptions, const char *keyword)
7398{
7399	PQconninfoOption *option;
7400
7401	for (option = connOptions; option->keyword != NULL; option++)
7402	{
7403		if (strcmp(option->keyword, keyword) == 0)
7404			return option;
7405	}
7406
7407	return NULL;
7408}
7409
7410
7411/*
7412 * Return the connection options used for the connection
7413 */
7414PQconninfoOption *
7415PQconninfo(PGconn *conn)
7416{
7417	PQExpBufferData errorBuf;
7418	PQconninfoOption *connOptions;
7419
7420	if (conn == NULL)
7421		return NULL;
7422
7423	/*
7424	 * We don't actually report any errors here, but callees want a buffer,
7425	 * and we prefer not to trash the conn's errorMessage.
7426	 */
7427	initPQExpBuffer(&errorBuf);
7428	if (PQExpBufferDataBroken(errorBuf))
7429		return NULL;			/* out of memory already :-( */
7430
7431	connOptions = conninfo_init(&errorBuf);
7432
7433	if (connOptions != NULL)
7434	{
7435		const internalPQconninfoOption *option;
7436
7437		for (option = PQconninfoOptions; option->keyword; option++)
7438		{
7439			char	  **connmember;
7440
7441			if (option->connofs < 0)
7442				continue;
7443
7444			connmember = (char **) ((char *) conn + option->connofs);
7445
7446			if (*connmember)
7447				conninfo_storeval(connOptions, option->keyword, *connmember,
7448								  &errorBuf, true, false);
7449		}
7450	}
7451
7452	termPQExpBuffer(&errorBuf);
7453
7454	return connOptions;
7455}
7456
7457
7458void
7459PQconninfoFree(PQconninfoOption *connOptions)
7460{
7461	if (connOptions == NULL)
7462		return;
7463
7464	for (PQconninfoOption *option = connOptions; option->keyword != NULL; option++)
7465		free(option->val);
7466	free(connOptions);
7467}
7468
7469
7470/* =========== accessor functions for PGconn ========= */
7471char *
7472PQdb(const PGconn *conn)
7473{
7474	if (!conn)
7475		return NULL;
7476	return conn->dbName;
7477}
7478
7479char *
7480PQuser(const PGconn *conn)
7481{
7482	if (!conn)
7483		return NULL;
7484	return conn->pguser;
7485}
7486
7487char *
7488PQpass(const PGconn *conn)
7489{
7490	char	   *password = NULL;
7491
7492	if (!conn)
7493		return NULL;
7494	if (conn->connhost != NULL)
7495		password = conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].password;
7496	if (password == NULL)
7497		password = conn->pgpass;
7498	/* Historically we've returned "" not NULL for no password specified */
7499	if (password == NULL)
7500		password = "";
7501	return password;
7502}
7503
7504char *
7505PQhost(const PGconn *conn)
7506{
7507	if (!conn)
7508		return NULL;
7509
7510	if (conn->connhost != NULL)
7511	{
7512		/*
7513		 * Return the verbatim host value provided by user, or hostaddr in its
7514		 * lack.
7515		 */
7516		if (conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].host != NULL &&
7517			conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].host[0] != '\0')
7518			return conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].host;
7519		else if (conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].hostaddr != NULL &&
7520				 conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].hostaddr[0] != '\0')
7521			return conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].hostaddr;
7522	}
7523
7524	return "";
7525}
7526
7527char *
7528PQhostaddr(const PGconn *conn)
7529{
7530	if (!conn)
7531		return NULL;
7532
7533	/* Return the parsed IP address */
7534	if (conn->connhost != NULL && conn->connip != NULL)
7535		return conn->connip;
7536
7537	return "";
7538}
7539
7540char *
7541PQport(const PGconn *conn)
7542{
7543	if (!conn)
7544		return NULL;
7545
7546	if (conn->connhost != NULL &&
7547		conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].port != NULL &&
7548		conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].port[0] != '\0')
7549		return conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].port;
7550
7551	return DEF_PGPORT_STR;
7552}
7553
7554/*
7555 * No longer does anything, but the function remains for API backwards
7556 * compatibility.
7557 */
7558char *
7559PQtty(const PGconn *conn)
7560{
7561	if (!conn)
7562		return NULL;
7563	return "";
7564}
7565
7566char *
7567PQoptions(const PGconn *conn)
7568{
7569	if (!conn)
7570		return NULL;
7571	return conn->pgoptions;
7572}
7573
7574ConnStatusType
7575PQstatus(const PGconn *conn)
7576{
7577	if (!conn)
7578		return CONNECTION_BAD;
7579	return conn->status;
7580}
7581
7582PGTransactionStatusType
7583PQtransactionStatus(const PGconn *conn)
7584{
7585	if (!conn || conn->status != CONNECTION_OK)
7586		return PQTRANS_UNKNOWN;
7587	if (conn->asyncStatus != PGASYNC_IDLE)
7588		return PQTRANS_ACTIVE;
7589	return conn->xactStatus;
7590}
7591
7592const char *
7593PQparameterStatus(const PGconn *conn, const char *paramName)
7594{
7595	const pgParameterStatus *pstatus;
7596
7597	if (!conn || !paramName)
7598		return NULL;
7599	for (pstatus = conn->pstatus; pstatus != NULL; pstatus = pstatus->next)
7600	{
7601		if (strcmp(pstatus->name, paramName) == 0)
7602			return pstatus->value;
7603	}
7604	return NULL;
7605}
7606
7607int
7608PQprotocolVersion(const PGconn *conn)
7609{
7610	if (!conn)
7611		return 0;
7612	if (conn->status == CONNECTION_BAD)
7613		return 0;
7614	return PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(conn->pversion);
7615}
7616
7617int
7618PQfullProtocolVersion(const PGconn *conn)
7619{
7620	if (!conn)
7621		return 0;
7622	if (conn->status == CONNECTION_BAD)
7623		return 0;
7624	return PG_PROTOCOL_FULL(conn->pversion);
7625}
7626
7627int
7628PQserverVersion(const PGconn *conn)
7629{
7630	if (!conn)
7631		return 0;
7632	if (conn->status == CONNECTION_BAD)
7633		return 0;
7634	return conn->sversion;
7635}
7636
7637char *
7638PQerrorMessage(const PGconn *conn)
7639{
7640	if (!conn)
7641		return libpq_gettext("connection pointer is NULL\n");
7642
7643	/*
7644	 * The errorMessage buffer might be marked "broken" due to having
7645	 * previously failed to allocate enough memory for the message.  In that
7646	 * case, tell the application we ran out of memory.
7647	 */
7648	if (PQExpBufferBroken(&conn->errorMessage))
7649		return libpq_gettext("out of memory\n");
7650
7651	return conn->errorMessage.data;
7652}
7653
7654/*
7655 * In Windows, socket values are unsigned, and an invalid socket value
7656 * (INVALID_SOCKET) is ~0, which equals -1 in comparisons (with no compiler
7657 * warning). Ideally we would return an unsigned value for PQsocket() on
7658 * Windows, but that would cause the function's return value to differ from
7659 * Unix, so we just return -1 for invalid sockets.
7660 * http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/cc507522%28v=vs.85%29.aspx
7661 * http://stackoverflow.com/questions/10817252/why-is-invalid-socket-defined-as-0-in-winsock2-h-c
7662 */
7663int
7664PQsocket(const PGconn *conn)
7665{
7666	if (!conn)
7667		return -1;
7668	if (conn->altsock != PGINVALID_SOCKET)
7669		return conn->altsock;
7670	return (conn->sock != PGINVALID_SOCKET) ? conn->sock : -1;
7671}
7672
7673int
7674PQbackendPID(const PGconn *conn)
7675{
7676	if (!conn || conn->status != CONNECTION_OK)
7677		return 0;
7678	return conn->be_pid;
7679}
7680
7681PGpipelineStatus
7682PQpipelineStatus(const PGconn *conn)
7683{
7684	if (!conn)
7685		return PQ_PIPELINE_OFF;
7686
7687	return conn->pipelineStatus;
7688}
7689
7690int
7691PQconnectionNeedsPassword(const PGconn *conn)
7692{
7693	char	   *password;
7694
7695	if (!conn)
7696		return false;
7697	password = PQpass(conn);
7698	if (conn->password_needed &&
7699		(password == NULL || password[0] == '\0'))
7700		return true;
7701	else
7702		return false;
7703}
7704
7705int
7706PQconnectionUsedPassword(const PGconn *conn)
7707{
7708	if (!conn)
7709		return false;
7710	if (conn->password_needed)
7711		return true;
7712	else
7713		return false;
7714}
7715
7716int
7717PQconnectionUsedGSSAPI(const PGconn *conn)
7718{
7719	if (!conn)
7720		return false;
7721	if (conn->gssapi_used)
7722		return true;
7723	else
7724		return false;
7725}
7726
7727int
7728PQclientEncoding(const PGconn *conn)
7729{
7730	if (!conn || conn->status != CONNECTION_OK)
7731		return -1;
7732	return conn->client_encoding;
7733}
7734
7735int
7736PQsetClientEncoding(PGconn *conn, const char *encoding)
7737{
7738	char		qbuf[128];
7739	static const char query[] = "set client_encoding to '%s'";
7740	PGresult   *res;
7741	int			status;
7742
7743	if (!conn || conn->status != CONNECTION_OK)
7744		return -1;
7745
7746	if (!encoding)
7747		return -1;
7748
7749	/* Resolve special "auto" value from the locale */
7750	if (strcmp(encoding, "auto") == 0)
7751		encoding = pg_encoding_to_char(pg_get_encoding_from_locale(NULL, true));
7752
7753	/* check query buffer overflow */
7754	if (sizeof(qbuf) < (sizeof(query) + strlen(encoding)))
7755		return -1;
7756
7757	/* ok, now send a query */
7758	sprintf(qbuf, query, encoding);
7759	res = PQexec(conn, qbuf);
7760
7761	if (res == NULL)
7762		return -1;
7763	if (res->resultStatus != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
7764		status = -1;
7765	else
7766	{
7767		/*
7768		 * We rely on the backend to report the parameter value, and we'll
7769		 * change state at that time.
7770		 */
7771		status = 0;				/* everything is ok */
7772	}
7773	PQclear(res);
7774	return status;
7775}
7776
7777PGVerbosity
7778PQsetErrorVerbosity(PGconn *conn, PGVerbosity verbosity)
7779{
7780	PGVerbosity old;
7781
7782	if (!conn)
7783		return PQERRORS_DEFAULT;
7784	old = conn->verbosity;
7785	conn->verbosity = verbosity;
7786	return old;
7787}
7788
7789PGContextVisibility
7790PQsetErrorContextVisibility(PGconn *conn, PGContextVisibility show_context)
7791{
7792	PGContextVisibility old;
7793
7794	if (!conn)
7795		return PQSHOW_CONTEXT_ERRORS;
7796	old = conn->show_context;
7797	conn->show_context = show_context;
7798	return old;
7799}
7800
7801PQnoticeReceiver
7802PQsetNoticeReceiver(PGconn *conn, PQnoticeReceiver proc, void *arg)
7803{
7804	PQnoticeReceiver old;
7805
7806	if (conn == NULL)
7807		return NULL;
7808
7809	old = conn->noticeHooks.noticeRec;
7810	if (proc)
7811	{
7812		conn->noticeHooks.noticeRec = proc;
7813		conn->noticeHooks.noticeRecArg = arg;
7814	}
7815	return old;
7816}
7817
7818PQnoticeProcessor
7819PQsetNoticeProcessor(PGconn *conn, PQnoticeProcessor proc, void *arg)
7820{
7821	PQnoticeProcessor old;
7822
7823	if (conn == NULL)
7824		return NULL;
7825
7826	old = conn->noticeHooks.noticeProc;
7827	if (proc)
7828	{
7829		conn->noticeHooks.noticeProc = proc;
7830		conn->noticeHooks.noticeProcArg = arg;
7831	}
7832	return old;
7833}
7834
7835/*
7836 * The default notice message receiver just gets the standard notice text
7837 * and sends it to the notice processor.  This two-level setup exists
7838 * mostly for backwards compatibility; perhaps we should deprecate use of
7839 * PQsetNoticeProcessor?
7840 */
7841static void
7842defaultNoticeReceiver(void *arg, const PGresult *res)
7843{
7844	(void) arg;					/* not used */
7845	if (res->noticeHooks.noticeProc != NULL)
7846		res->noticeHooks.noticeProc(res->noticeHooks.noticeProcArg,
7847									PQresultErrorMessage(res));
7848}
7849
7850/*
7851 * The default notice message processor just prints the
7852 * message on stderr.  Applications can override this if they
7853 * want the messages to go elsewhere (a window, for example).
7854 * Note that simply discarding notices is probably a bad idea.
7855 */
7856static void
7857defaultNoticeProcessor(void *arg, const char *message)
7858{
7859	(void) arg;					/* not used */
7860	/* Note: we expect the supplied string to end with a newline already. */
7861	fprintf(stderr, "%s", message);
7862}
7863
7864/*
7865 * returns a pointer to the next token or NULL if the current
7866 * token doesn't match
7867 */
7868static char *
7869pwdfMatchesString(char *buf, const char *token)
7870{
7871	char	   *tbuf;
7872	const char *ttok;
7873	bool		bslash = false;
7874
7875	if (buf == NULL || token == NULL)
7876		return NULL;
7877	tbuf = buf;
7878	ttok = token;
7879	if (tbuf[0] == '*' && tbuf[1] == ':')
7880		return tbuf + 2;
7881	while (*tbuf != 0)
7882	{
7883		if (*tbuf == '\\' && !bslash)
7884		{
7885			tbuf++;
7886			bslash = true;
7887		}
7888		if (*tbuf == ':' && *ttok == 0 && !bslash)
7889			return tbuf + 1;
7890		bslash = false;
7891		if (*ttok == 0)
7892			return NULL;
7893		if (*tbuf == *ttok)
7894		{
7895			tbuf++;
7896			ttok++;
7897		}
7898		else
7899			return NULL;
7900	}
7901	return NULL;
7902}
7903
7904/* Get a password from the password file. Return value is malloc'd. */
7905static char *
7906passwordFromFile(const char *hostname, const char *port, const char *dbname,
7907				 const char *username, const char *pgpassfile)
7908{
7909	FILE	   *fp;
7910#ifndef WIN32
7911	struct stat stat_buf;
7912#endif
7913	PQExpBufferData buf;
7914
7915	if (dbname == NULL || dbname[0] == '\0')
7916		return NULL;
7917
7918	if (username == NULL || username[0] == '\0')
7919		return NULL;
7920
7921	/* 'localhost' matches pghost of '' or the default socket directory */
7922	if (hostname == NULL || hostname[0] == '\0')
7923		hostname = DefaultHost;
7924	else if (is_unixsock_path(hostname))
7925
7926		/*
7927		 * We should probably use canonicalize_path(), but then we have to
7928		 * bring path.c into libpq, and it doesn't seem worth it.
7929		 */
7930		if (strcmp(hostname, DEFAULT_PGSOCKET_DIR) == 0)
7931			hostname = DefaultHost;
7932
7933	if (port == NULL || port[0] == '\0')
7934		port = DEF_PGPORT_STR;
7935
7936	/* If password file cannot be opened, ignore it. */
7937	fp = fopen(pgpassfile, "r");
7938	if (fp == NULL)
7939		return NULL;
7940
7941#ifndef WIN32
7942	if (fstat(fileno(fp), &stat_buf) != 0)
7943	{
7944		fclose(fp);
7945		return NULL;
7946	}
7947
7948	if (!S_ISREG(stat_buf.st_mode))
7949	{
7950		fprintf(stderr,
7951				libpq_gettext("WARNING: password file \"%s\" is not a plain file\n"),
7952				pgpassfile);
7953		fclose(fp);
7954		return NULL;
7955	}
7956
7957	/* If password file is insecure, alert the user and ignore it. */
7958	if (stat_buf.st_mode & (S_IRWXG | S_IRWXO))
7959	{
7960		fprintf(stderr,
7961				libpq_gettext("WARNING: password file \"%s\" has group or world access; permissions should be u=rw (0600) or less\n"),
7962				pgpassfile);
7963		fclose(fp);
7964		return NULL;
7965	}
7966#else
7967
7968	/*
7969	 * On Win32, the directory is protected, so we don't have to check the
7970	 * file.
7971	 */
7972#endif
7973
7974	/* Use an expansible buffer to accommodate any reasonable line length */
7975	initPQExpBuffer(&buf);
7976
7977	while (!feof(fp) && !ferror(fp))
7978	{
7979		/* Make sure there's a reasonable amount of room in the buffer */
7980		if (!enlargePQExpBuffer(&buf, 128))
7981			break;
7982
7983		/* Read some data, appending it to what we already have */
7984		if (fgets(buf.data + buf.len, buf.maxlen - buf.len, fp) == NULL)
7985			break;
7986		buf.len += strlen(buf.data + buf.len);
7987
7988		/* If we don't yet have a whole line, loop around to read more */
7989		if (!(buf.len > 0 && buf.data[buf.len - 1] == '\n') && !feof(fp))
7990			continue;
7991
7992		/* ignore comments */
7993		if (buf.data[0] != '#')
7994		{
7995			char	   *t = buf.data;
7996			int			len;
7997
7998			/* strip trailing newline and carriage return */
7999			len = pg_strip_crlf(t);
8000
8001			if (len > 0 &&
8002				(t = pwdfMatchesString(t, hostname)) != NULL &&
8003				(t = pwdfMatchesString(t, port)) != NULL &&
8004				(t = pwdfMatchesString(t, dbname)) != NULL &&
8005				(t = pwdfMatchesString(t, username)) != NULL)
8006			{
8007				/* Found a match. */
8008				char	   *ret,
8009						   *p1,
8010						   *p2;
8011
8012				ret = strdup(t);
8013
8014				fclose(fp);
8015				explicit_bzero(buf.data, buf.maxlen);
8016				termPQExpBuffer(&buf);
8017
8018				if (!ret)
8019				{
8020					/* Out of memory. XXX: an error message would be nice. */
8021					return NULL;
8022				}
8023
8024				/* De-escape password. */
8025				for (p1 = p2 = ret; *p1 != ':' && *p1 != '\0'; ++p1, ++p2)
8026				{
8027					if (*p1 == '\\' && p1[1] != '\0')
8028						++p1;
8029					*p2 = *p1;
8030				}
8031				*p2 = '\0';
8032
8033				return ret;
8034			}
8035		}
8036
8037		/* No match, reset buffer to prepare for next line. */
8038		buf.len = 0;
8039	}
8040
8041	fclose(fp);
8042	explicit_bzero(buf.data, buf.maxlen);
8043	termPQExpBuffer(&buf);
8044	return NULL;
8045}
8046
8047
8048/*
8049 *	If the connection failed due to bad password, we should mention
8050 *	if we got the password from the pgpassfile.
8051 */
8052static void
8053pgpassfileWarning(PGconn *conn)
8054{
8055	/* If it was 'invalid authorization', add pgpassfile mention */
8056	/* only works with >= 9.0 servers */
8057	if (conn->password_needed &&
8058		conn->connhost[conn->whichhost].password != NULL &&
8059		conn->result)
8060	{
8061		const char *sqlstate = PQresultErrorField(conn->result,
8062												  PG_DIAG_SQLSTATE);
8063
8064		if (sqlstate && strcmp(sqlstate, ERRCODE_INVALID_PASSWORD) == 0)
8065			libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "password retrieved from file \"%s\"",
8066									conn->pgpassfile);
8067	}
8068}
8069
8070/*
8071 * Check if the SSL protocol value given in input is valid or not.
8072 * This is used as a sanity check routine for the connection parameters
8073 * ssl_min_protocol_version and ssl_max_protocol_version.
8074 */
8075static bool
8076sslVerifyProtocolVersion(const char *version)
8077{
8078	/*
8079	 * An empty string and a NULL value are considered valid as it is
8080	 * equivalent to ignoring the parameter.
8081	 */
8082	if (!version || strlen(version) == 0)
8083		return true;
8084
8085	if (pg_strcasecmp(version, "TLSv1") == 0 ||
8086		pg_strcasecmp(version, "TLSv1.1") == 0 ||
8087		pg_strcasecmp(version, "TLSv1.2") == 0 ||
8088		pg_strcasecmp(version, "TLSv1.3") == 0)
8089		return true;
8090
8091	/* anything else is wrong */
8092	return false;
8093}
8094
8095
8096/*
8097 * Ensure that the SSL protocol range given in input is correct.  The check
8098 * is performed on the input string to keep it TLS backend agnostic.  Input
8099 * to this function is expected verified with sslVerifyProtocolVersion().
8100 */
8101static bool
8102sslVerifyProtocolRange(const char *min, const char *max)
8103{
8104	Assert(sslVerifyProtocolVersion(min) &&
8105		   sslVerifyProtocolVersion(max));
8106
8107	/* If at least one of the bounds is not set, the range is valid */
8108	if (min == NULL || max == NULL || strlen(min) == 0 || strlen(max) == 0)
8109		return true;
8110
8111	/*
8112	 * If the minimum version is the lowest one we accept, then all options
8113	 * for the maximum are valid.
8114	 */
8115	if (pg_strcasecmp(min, "TLSv1") == 0)
8116		return true;
8117
8118	/*
8119	 * The minimum bound is valid, and cannot be TLSv1, so using TLSv1 for the
8120	 * maximum is incorrect.
8121	 */
8122	if (pg_strcasecmp(max, "TLSv1") == 0)
8123		return false;
8124
8125	/*
8126	 * At this point we know that we have a mix of TLSv1.1 through 1.3
8127	 * versions.
8128	 */
8129	if (pg_strcasecmp(min, max) > 0)
8130		return false;
8131
8132	return true;
8133}
8134
8135
8136/*
8137 * Obtain user's home directory, return in given buffer
8138 *
8139 * On Unix, this actually returns the user's home directory.  On Windows
8140 * it returns the PostgreSQL-specific application data folder.
8141 *
8142 * This is essentially the same as get_home_path(), but we don't use that
8143 * because we don't want to pull path.c into libpq (it pollutes application
8144 * namespace).
8145 *
8146 * Returns true on success, false on failure to obtain the directory name.
8147 *
8148 * CAUTION: although in most situations failure is unexpected, there are users
8149 * who like to run applications in a home-directory-less environment.  On
8150 * failure, you almost certainly DO NOT want to report an error.  Just act as
8151 * though whatever file you were hoping to find in the home directory isn't
8152 * there (which it isn't).
8153 */
8154bool
8155pqGetHomeDirectory(char *buf, int bufsize)
8156{
8157#ifndef WIN32
8158	const char *home;
8159
8160	home = getenv("HOME");
8161	if (home && home[0])
8162	{
8163		strlcpy(buf, home, bufsize);
8164		return true;
8165	}
8166	else
8167	{
8168		struct passwd pwbuf;
8169		struct passwd *pw;
8170		char		tmpbuf[1024];
8171		int			rc;
8172
8173		rc = getpwuid_r(geteuid(), &pwbuf, tmpbuf, sizeof tmpbuf, &pw);
8174		if (rc != 0 || !pw)
8175			return false;
8176		strlcpy(buf, pw->pw_dir, bufsize);
8177		return true;
8178	}
8179#else
8180	char		tmppath[MAX_PATH];
8181
8182	ZeroMemory(tmppath, sizeof(tmppath));
8183	if (SHGetFolderPath(NULL, CSIDL_APPDATA, NULL, 0, tmppath) != S_OK)
8184		return false;
8185	snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s/postgresql", tmppath);
8186	return true;
8187#endif
8188}
8189
8190/*
8191 * Parse and try to interpret "value" as an integer value, and if successful,
8192 * store it in *result, complaining if there is any trailing garbage or an
8193 * overflow.  This allows any number of leading and trailing whitespaces.
8194 */
8195bool
8196pqParseIntParam(const char *value, int *result, PGconn *conn,
8197				const char *context)
8198{
8199	char	   *end;
8200	long		numval;
8201
8202	Assert(value != NULL);
8203
8204	*result = 0;
8205
8206	/* strtol(3) skips leading whitespaces */
8207	errno = 0;
8208	numval = strtol(value, &end, 10);
8209
8210	/*
8211	 * If no progress was done during the parsing or an error happened, fail.
8212	 * This tests properly for overflows of the result.
8213	 */
8214	if (value == end || errno != 0 || numval != (int) numval)
8215		goto error;
8216
8217	/*
8218	 * Skip any trailing whitespace; if anything but whitespace remains before
8219	 * the terminating character, fail
8220	 */
8221	while (*end != '\0' && isspace((unsigned char) *end))
8222		end++;
8223
8224	if (*end != '\0')
8225		goto error;
8226
8227	*result = numval;
8228	return true;
8229
8230error:
8231	libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid integer value \"%s\" for connection option \"%s\"",
8232							value, context);
8233	return false;
8234}
8235
8236/*
8237 * Parse and try to interpret "value" as a ProtocolVersion value, and if
8238 * successful, store it in *result.
8239 */
8240static bool
8241pqParseProtocolVersion(const char *value, ProtocolVersion *result, PGconn *conn,
8242					   const char *context)
8243{
8244	if (strcmp(value, "latest") == 0)
8245	{
8246		*result = PG_PROTOCOL_LATEST;
8247		return true;
8248	}
8249	if (strcmp(value, "3.0") == 0)
8250	{
8251		*result = PG_PROTOCOL(3, 0);
8252		return true;
8253	}
8254
8255	/* 3.1 never existed, we went straight from 3.0 to 3.2 */
8256
8257	if (strcmp(value, "3.2") == 0)
8258	{
8259		*result = PG_PROTOCOL(3, 2);
8260		return true;
8261	}
8262
8263	libpq_append_conn_error(conn, "invalid %s value: \"%s\"",
8264							context, value);
8265	return false;
8266}
8267
8268/*
8269 * To keep the API consistent, the locking stubs are always provided, even
8270 * if they are not required.
8271 *
8272 * Since we neglected to provide any error-return convention in the
8273 * pgthreadlock_t API, we can't do much except Assert upon failure of any
8274 * mutex primitive.  Fortunately, such failures appear to be nonexistent in
8275 * the field.
8276 */
8277
8278static void
8279default_threadlock(int acquire)
8280{
8281	static pthread_mutex_t singlethread_lock = PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER;
8282
8283	if (acquire)
8284	{
8285		if (pthread_mutex_lock(&singlethread_lock))
8286			Assert(false);
8287	}
8288	else
8289	{
8290		if (pthread_mutex_unlock(&singlethread_lock))
8291			Assert(false);
8292	}
8293}
8294
8295pgthreadlock_t
8296PQregisterThreadLock(pgthreadlock_t newhandler)
8297{
8298	pgthreadlock_t prev = pg_g_threadlock;
8299
8300	if (newhandler)
8301		pg_g_threadlock = newhandler;
8302	else
8303		pg_g_threadlock = default_threadlock;
8304
8305	return prev;
8306}
